jobs.title: Digital Content Specialist, Hybrid, UC Online
jobs.description: **Current UC employees must apply internally via _SuccessFactors_** **Next Lives at the University of Cincinnati** Founded in 1819, the University of Cincinnati ranks among the nation’s best urban public research universities. Home to more than 53,600 students, 12,000 faculty and staff, and over 360,000 living alumni, UC, a Carnegie 1 institution, combines research prowess with a physical setting The New York Times has called “the most ambitious campus design program in the country." UC's momentum has never been stronger as the anchor of the Cincinnati Innovation District, the oldest cooperative education (co-op) program in the country with students earning $94 million annually through paid experiences, an academic health system, and as a member of the Big 12 Conference. The university contributes $10.6 billion in economic impact to the city and $22.7 billion to the state of Ohio. At UC, next is all of us. Learn more at[uc.edu](http://uc.edu/). UC is a mission-driven organization where we are committed to student success and positively transforming the community through scholarship and service. We thrive on innovation, making an impact, and fostering an environment where staff and faculty are key contributors to UC’s success. **About UC Online** ------------------- UC Online, a division of the University of Cincinnati, delivers a top-tier educational experience for students seeking the flexibility of online learning paired with the excellence of UC’s nationally recognized programs. Our focus on quality and innovation supports students throughout their entire academic journey—from initial inquiry to graduation. Our mission is rooted in student success. We use data-driven strategies to help students identify the right programs, stay engaged in their coursework, and reach their academic and career goals. At UC Online, we empower learners to build brighter futures through practical, research-informed education from a university known for its history of innovation and achievement. This position follows a hybrid work model for candidates residing in Ohio, Indiana, or Kentucky. Candidates must live within the Cincinnati Tri-State area and be able to commute to UC’s Main Campus as needed. Remote work is governed by university policy and may be adjusted accordingly. **Job Overview** ---------------- The Digital Content Specialist plays a central role in shaping, writing, editing, and publishing content across UC Online’s digital ecosystem. This role blends strong copywriting and editorial skills with content operations, accessibility, and brand governance. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys writing original content, refining voice and tone, and translating complex academic and institutional information into clear, compelling, audience-centered messaging. The role also supports publishing workflows, content standards, and collaboration across centralized and decentralized teams. **Essential Functions** ----------------------- * Write, edit, and publish original digital content, including blogs, feature stories, and long-form editorial pieces that support brand positioning, SEO, and learner engagement. * Create and adapt content for distribution across multiple channels, including websites, email marketing, and social media, following established brand and style guidelines. * Contribute regularly to UC Online’s blog and editorial content calendar, supporting content development from ideation through publication in collaboration with senior content staff. * Review, copyedit, and publish digital content to ensure clarity, consistency, accuracy, and alignment with brand voice and accessibility standards. * Translate complex academic, programmatic, and institutional information into clear, accessible, and engaging copy for prospective and current learners. * Assist with updating and refreshing existing content to ensure relevance, accuracy, and performance over time. * Support accessibility compliance by applying WCAG standards and Sections 504 and 508 requirements to digital content and collaborate with technical and communications teams. * Provide CMS support and assistance to web content editors as directed, following established workflows and processes. * Support coordination of digital editorial schedules and content updates as assigned. * Manage assigned content management system tasks and perform related duties based on departmental needs. * Perform related duties based on departmental need. This job description can be changed at any time. **Required Education** ---------------------- * Bachelor’s Degree. * Five (5) years of relevant work experience and/or other specialized training can be used in lieu of education requirement. **Required Experience** ----------------------- * Experience with web content management systems and digital publishing workflows. * Working knowledge of HTML, CSS and Adobe Creative Suite. * Understanding of Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WC

This job description can be changed at any time. **Required Education** ---------------------- * Bachelor’s Degree. * Five (5) years of relevant work experience and/or other specialized training can be used in lieu of education requirement. **Required Experience** ----------------------- * Experience with web content management systems and digital publishing workflows. * Working knowledge of HTML, CSS and Adobe Creative Suite. * Understanding of Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG). **Additional Qualifications Considered** ---------------------------------------- * One or more years of experience in copywriting, editorial writing, social media, content creation, or content management for digital platforms. * Demonstrated ability to write and edit clear, engaging long-form content (blogs, articles, features, or similar) and short-form content (social media). * Experience writing and creating content for higher education, mission-driven organizations, or complex institutions. * Familiarity with SEO best practices and content performance measurement. * Strong editorial judgment and the ability to balance creativity with brand and compliance requirements. **

jobs.title: Web Developer (AZ Local)
jobs.description: **Job Title:** Web Developer (Hybrid) **Job Code:** AZ 9828 **Client:** Arizona State Parks & Trails (AZSTATEPARKS) **Location:** Phoenix, AZ (Hybrid) **Duration:** Contract through 09/09/2026 **Hours:** Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (40 hrs/week) **Work Address:** 1110 W Washington St., Phoenix, AZ 85007 **Important Requirements** * **Local Arizona candidates only** * Current location must be clearly mentioned on resume * Must be available for **in-person interview within 1 week** of posting close date * Must be able to **start within 2 weeks** of offer * Position is **Hybrid** (Primarily remote, occasional in-office meetings) **Position Overview** Arizona State Parks and Trails (ASPT) is seeking a Web Developer to support and maintain **azstateparks.com**, a high-traffic website receiving over **14 million visits annually**, along with related reservation pages. The developer will focus heavily on **WCAG 2.2 accessibility compliance**, CMS updates, performance improvements, and collaboration with Tyler Technologies to resolve system-level issues. **Core Technical Skills** * HTML & CSS * JavaScript * Responsive Web Design * Git (Version Control) * Backend & API Integration (Node.js, SQL, Databases) * Testing & Debugging * Web Performance Optimization * Security Best Practices **Required Experience** * Strong experience with **HTML & CSS** * Hands-on **JavaScript** development experience * Backend and API integration experience (**Node.js, SQL, etc.**) * Experience working with **databases** * Familiarity with **WCAG accessibility standards** * Experience using code repositories (Git) **Scope of Work** Under the supervision of IT and Communications leadership, the contractor will: * Use **Acquia Optimize** to identify accessibility and quality issues * Implement **WCAG 2.2 A & AA fixes** within the CMS * Fix broken links and broken images * Compile issues requiring vendor (Tyler Technologies) intervention * Monitor accessibility improvements and recommend enhancements * Coordinate with Tyler Technologies on releases and validate functionality post-release Currently identified: * 48 Level A accessibility issues * 24 Level AA accessibility issues **About the Environment** * Custom CMS developed by **Tyler Technologies** * Accessibility scanning tools: **Level Access & Acquia Optimize** * High-visibility public-facing government website * Collaboration with Communications, Research & Marketing (CRAM) team **Ideal Candidate** * Strong understanding of web accessibility (WCAG 2.2 A & AA) * Comfortable working in a structured government environment * Detail-oriented with strong debugging and research skills * Able to collaborate with third-party vendors and internal stakeholders Compensation: $51.00 per hour About SR International INC. --------------------------- SR International has been a leading name among the IT consulting companies with offices in US and India. For past 16 years, our industry experience and domain knowledge have enabled us to provide innovative solutions to our customers. **Who We Are** We Are Leading IT Based Solution Providers ----------------------------------------- Today, the world of business information represents the realization of our collective efforts toward improving the future. Held only by the limits of our imagination, the business world is accelerating at an ever-increasing pace. Imagine a better way of doing business, of implementing the perfect software, of refining practice or business integration. All it takes are benchmark standards in service, support, and technical know-how, which have been our bread and butter. **Our Vision.** Established in 2002, SR International Inc is one of the fastest growing and reputed provider of Information Technology Services and Solutions in the USA. Since our inception, we have been a trusted IT partner for our clients. We take pride in our highly skilled IT Resources and unique engagement model. We have been consistently delivering on our promises as a high-performance team. Our expertise in Cloud Computing, Mobility, Web Technologies, ERP and CRM are second to none. Our industry-leading flagship product iMathSmart is re-defining math learning experience for school students. Career At SR International -------------------------- At SR International, we treat our consultants like family. Our business and our reputation have been built and maintained by quality resources working onboard, so it’s important for us to maintain the quality resource pool. Apply with Indeed (if you already have a resume on Indeed) URL: https://app.careerplug.com/jobs/3272671/apps/new

jobs.title: UI Developer Design Systems
jobs.description: UI Developer / Front End Engineer (UI Design Systems) Sunderland / WFH to £50k Are you a creative UI Developer with a strong appreciation of responsive UI design? You could be progressing your career at a growing games / gambling site, whilst enjoying a range of benefits and perks. As a UI Developer you'll collaborate closely with UI Designers, bridging the gap between design and engineering to build reusable UI components that present a cohesive visual style and deliver consistent customer experiences. You'll own the architecture, development and evolution of the design system, empowering teams across the organisation to build interfaces rapidly and reliably, championing accessibility and design best practices throughout the development process. Location / WFH: There's a hybrid work from home policy with 2-3 days a week; when you're in the office you'll be collaborating with fellow technologists in a relaxed environment in awesome custom built offices in Sunderland with a range of facilities and perks including free meals at the onsite restaurant as well as membership at onsite gym. About you: * You have experience with modern front end tools and development languages including TypeScript, Vite, Lit, HTML, CSS, PostCSS, SASS * You have experience with component driven architecture * You have a deep understanding of responsive design and accessibility standards, e.g. WCAG, ARIA * You keep up to date with best practices and emerging technologies within front end engineering * You're collaborative with great communication skills What's in it for you: As a UI Developer / Front End Engineer you will receive a competitive package: * Competitive salary to £55k + quarterly bonus * Holiday purchase scheme, plus an extra day for every your there (up to 5) * Pension, Private medical care * Onsite gym * Onsite restaurant with free breakfast, lunch, snacks and drinks * Free inner city parking * Free tickets to EFL matches * Hybrid working (x3 days a week office) * Family friendly policies * Social events and more! Apply now to find out more about this UI Developer / Front End Engineer (UI Design Systems) opportunity. At Client Server we believe in a diverse workplace that allows people to play to their strengths and continually learn. We're an equal opportunities employer whose people come from all walks of life and will never discriminate based on race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. The clients we work with share our values.

jobs.title: Product Designer (Remote)
jobs.description: Our People Strategy & Operations team elevates Cisco's services, products, and solutions by championing a user-centered mindset. We strengthen design capabilities and empower others through maturing design systems, UX research, and upholding high standards. This hands-on role implements and optimizes digital experiences on platforms like Phenom. You'll contribute to transformational projects across the entire employee journey, including AI initiatives, and manage Cisco's Career Sites to enhance People Experiences. Our global, collaborative team works with diverse stakeholders to redefine experiences, starting from careers.cisco.com. **Join us and make a real impact on the future of work at Cisco!** * A portfolio is mandatory. Please include a link to your online portfolio with your application. Your Impact * Champion User-Centricity & Implementation: Advocate for and apply a user-centered approach throughout product development, partnering closely with product management and engineering. You'll not only design but also be responsible for the hands-on development and maintenance of pages within our Phenom CMS for the Career Sites, including bug fixes and continuous improvement. * Drive Design Execution: Translate requirements into tangible design work, including flows, wireframes, interactive prototypes, and visual designs for individual product features. * Enhance Customer Experience: Apply or support the creation of discovery UX artifacts (like personas and journey maps) to continuously improve the customer experience across our portfolio. * Ensure Quality & Accessibility: Prioritize ease of use, high quality, and digital accessibility (#a11y) in all product solutions. * End-to-End Design Process: Facilitate workshops to inspire innovation and extract the best ideas, validate and improve designs through user feedback, create crucial design resources like templates and guidelines, and present persuasive, user-focused design concepts to the team. **What You’ll Learn and Work On in the Future:** * Career Sites Evolution: Your main focus will be on our Career Sites, involving bug resolution, designing and developing new pages within the Phenom CMS, and overall maintenance and improvement based on user testing and feedback. * Experience Design: Craft impactful experiences using design thinking, prototyping, agile development, and UX principles. * SaaS Integration: Gain an understanding of how these experiences integrate with Cisco’s next-generation platforms (e.g. Phenom, Workday, and ServiceNow). * AI-Enhanced Innovation: Continuously discover and implement new AI-enhanced technologies and approaches to boost our efficiency, quality, and scalability. **Minimum Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree with 2 years of related experience, or Master's degree with 0 years of related experience. * Possess strong foundational UX Skills and Capabilities including Interaction Design, Information Architecture, Visual Design, User Research, Content Design, and UI Design. * Proficiency in modern design tools like Figma, Miro, Qualtrics, Maze, etc. * Hands-on experience conducting UX research to gather insights and validate designs. * Demonstrated experience with Content Management Systems (CMS) or similar web publishing platforms. * Basic understanding of front-end web technologies (HTML, CSS concepts) to effectively implement designs and collaborate with engineers. * In-depth knowledge of web accessibility standards (e.g., WCAG) and experience designing inclusive experiences. * Strong organizational, project, and time management skills including using tools like Airtable and ServiceNow. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate design decisions and rationale clearly. * A growth and user-centered mindset with an insatiable appetite for continuous learning. **Preferred Qualifications** * Advanced proficiency in Figma's advanced features (e.g., Figma Make, Figma MCP Server). * Experience working with and contributing to Design Systems built in Figma. * Direct experience building, maintaining, and optimizing Career Sites or other web experiences using SaaS products like Phenom or Workday Recruiting. * Experience in working with defects in an Agile team. * Experience designing for HR technology, talent acquisition, or other people-focused platforms. * Demonstrable understanding of UX writing principles and content strategy. * Familiarity with web analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics) to inform and measure design impact. * Exposure to or interest in designing for AI/ML-powered features or understanding ethical AI design principles. Why Cisco? At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds

Why Cisco? At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint. Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere. We are Cisco, and our power starts with you. Message to applicants applying to work in the U.S. and/or Canada: The starting salary range posted for this position is $95,700.00 to $131,200.00 and reflects the projected salary range for new hires in this position in U.S. and/or Canada locations, not including incentive compensation*, equity, or

jobs.title: Lead UX Designer - Agentic AI Platform
jobs.description: Lead UX Designer - Agentic AI Platform Mechanicsburg, PA Posted today [Apply Now](https://rr.jobsyn.org/70EF298A164F47998E9165F7C176D74510) Zora AI is seeking a Senior UX Designer to lead end-to-end product design for GenAI-powered experiences, translating complex model capabilities into simple, trustworthy workflows. You'll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Research, Data Science/ML, and Risk/Legal to deliver usable, accessible, and governable user experiences across web and/or enterprise platforms. Recruiting for this role ends on May 15th 2026. Key Responsibilities: * Own UX for key Zora AI product areas: discovery → definition → design → validation → launch → iteration. * Translate product strategy and model capabilities into user journeys, information architecture, and interaction patterns. * Design AI-specific experiences: prompt/input patterns, outputs, citations/provenance, feedback loops, error states, and safe fallback behaviors. * Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs in a scalable system (components, patterns, tokens). * Lead and/or partner on user research (concept tests, usability, diary studies) and synthesize insights into design decisions. * Define and measure UX success metrics (task success, time-on-task, comprehension, trust, adoption, deflection). * Collaborate with ML/engineering to align on model constraints, latency, confidence, and evaluation signals that impact UX. * Ensure experiences meet accessibility (WCAG) and enterprise requirements (security, permissions, auditability). * Contribute to and evolve the design system and content standards (UX writing for AI, tone, disclaimers). * Manage the UX team's work and mentor UX designers on the team and elevate design quality via critiques, coaching, and pragmatic craft standards. Required Qualifications: * 5+ years of UX/Product Design experience delivering shipped digital products. * Fluency in interaction design, visual design, and prototyping; strong design critique and storytelling skills. * 1+ year designing for GenAI/AI features (or adjacent complex systems) with clear rationale. * Strong portfolio demonstrating complex workflow design (enterprise, analytics, or productivity preferred). * Limited immigration sponsorship may be available * Ability to travel 0-10%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve Preferred: * Experience designing LLM-based assistants, agentic workflows, retrieval/search, or evaluation/feedback systems. * Experience with Figma Make or other prompt based prototyping tools * Ability to lead junior UX designers and delegate work to balance multiple priorities. * Experience working in cross-functional teams with product managers, engineers, and ideally data/ML partners. * Comfort navigating ambiguity, making tradeoffs, and driving alignment with stakeholders. * Practical knowledge of accessibility and inclusive design patterns. * Familiarity with trust, safety, and governance UX (human-in-the-loop, audit trails, permissions). * Background in service design or end-to-end journey mapping across channels. * Experience with regulated or risk-sensitive environments (e.g., financial services, healthcare, public sector). Key Deliverables * Journey maps, service blueprints, task flows * IA/navigation models, wireframes, interactive prototypes * High-fidelity UI, responsive specs, component contributions * Research plans/readouts, usability findings, design decision logs * Metrics definitions and post-launch iteration plans How success will be measured (example outcomes) * Increased task completion and reduced time-to-complete core workflows * Improved user trust/comprehension of AI outputs (measured via tests/surveys/behavior) * Reduced fallback/escalation rates through better guidance and error recovery * Faster delivery via reusable patterns and a stronger design system Working model & stakeholders (edit as needed) * Reports to: Product Portfolio Leaders (Finance & Procurement) * Partners with: Product Management, Engineering, Compliance/Risk, QA, Commercialization The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $80,000 - $165,000. You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby

jobs.title: Dashboard Accessibility Analyst - Intermittent (Management Analyst)
jobs.description: THIS POSITION IS INTERMITTENT & IS NOT TO EXCEED 1,000 HOURS IN A STATE FISCAL YEAR. **About Us:** Investing in opportunities for Ohioans that work for every person and every family in every corner of our state is at the hallmark of Governor DeWine’s agenda for Ohio’s future. To ensure Ohio is “the best place to live, work, raise and family and start a business,” we must have strong schools, a great quality of life, and compassion for those who need our help. Ohio Department of Medicaid plays a unique and necessary role in supporting the governor’s vision. As the single state Medicaid agency responsible for administering high-quality, person-centric healthcare, the department is committed to supporting the health and wellbeing of nearly one in every four Ohioans served. We do so by: * Delivering a personalized care experience to more than three million people served. * Improving care for children and adults with complex behavioral health needs. * Working collectively with our partners and providers to measurably strengthen wellness and health outcomes. * Streamlining administrative burdens so doctors and healthcare providers have more time for patient care. * Ensuring financial transparency and operational accountability across all Medicaid programs and services. **What You Will Do at ODM:** **Office:** Data & Integrated Systems **Bureau:** Strategic & Performance Analytics **Classification:** Management Analyst (PN 20100186) **Job Overview:** The Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) is seeking a Dashboard Accessibility Analyst to ensure all public-facing dashboards and visualizations meet WCAG 2.1 AA and ADA accessibility standards. This role is critical in making data visualizations accessible to all users by embedding alt text, managing accessibility processes, and monitoring compliance. **Key Responsibilities** * Accessibility Compliance: Ensure dashboards and visualizations meet WCAG 2.1 AA and ADA standards. * Alt Text Development: Write and embed clear, meaningful alt text for all dashboard visualizations. * Accessibility Reviews: Review dashboards for compliance and usability; identify and recommend improvements. * Documentation: Develop guidelines, checklists, and best practices for creating accessible dashboards. * Collaboration: Work with Data Analysts, Developers, Designers, and Content Creators to integrate accessibility features. * Operational Support: Provide guidance to staff on accessibility standards and practices **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of WCAG 2.1 AA standards, ADA compliance, and accessibility principles. * Familiarity with data visualization software (e.g. Tableau, Power BI, Qlik). Why Work for the State of Ohio At the State of Ohio, we take care of the team that cares for Ohioans. We provide a variety of quality, competitive benefits to eligible full-time and part-time employees*. For a list of all the State of Ohio Benefits, visit our[Total Rewards website](https://careers.ohio.gov/why-work-for-state-of-ohio)! Our benefits package includes: * Medical Coverage * Free Dental, Vision and Basic Life Insurance premiums after completion of [eligibility period](https://careers.ohio.gov/why-work-for-state-of-ohio/healthcare/01-health-care)[](https://careers.ohio.gov/why-work-for-state-of-ohio/healthcare/01-health-care) * Paid time off, including vacation, personal, sick leave and 11 paid holidays per year * Childbirth, Adoption, and Foster Care leave * Education and Development Opportunities (Employee Development Funds, Public Service Loan Forgiveness, and more) * Public Retirement Systems ([such as OPERS, STRS, SERS, and HPRS](https://das.ohio.gov/employee-relations/benefits-administration/about-my-benefits/retirement-systems))&Optional Deferred Compensation ([Ohio Deferred Compensation](https://www.ohio457.org/aboutus)) *Benefits eligibility is dependent on a number of factors. The Agency Contact listed above will be able to provide specific benefits information for this position. 2 yrs. trg. or 2 yrs. exp. in research & analysis & written documentation of findings. -Or completion of undergraduate core program in business or public administration or related field of study. -Or equivalent of Minimum Class Qualifications For Employment noted above. **Job Skills:** Operational and Administrative Support **Technical Skills:** Data Visualization **Professional Skills:** Attention to Detail, Proofreading, Collaboration Supplemental Information THIS POSITION IS UNCLASSIFIED PER 124.11 (A) (29), OHIO REVISED CODE. The Ohio Department of Medicaid does not provide employment-based visa sponsorship. Compensation is as listed on the posting unless required by legislation or union contract. Travel required, as needed. Must provide own transportation. Or, in order to operate a state vehicle, you must have a valid driver's license from state of residence. Resumes and/or attachments are not reviewed. Therefore, please provide detail in the work experienc

jobs.title: Senior SDET - Accessibility
jobs.description: Sprinklr is the definitive, AI-native platform for Unified Customer Experience Management (Unified-CXM), empowering brands to deliver extraordinary experiences at scale — across every customer touchpoint. By combining human instinct with the speed and efficiency of AI, Sprinklr helps brands earn trust and loyalty through personalized, seamless, and efficient customer interactions. Sprinklr’s unified platform provides powerful solutions for every customer-facing team — spanning social media management, marketing, advertising, customer feedback, and omnichannel contact center management — enabling enterprises to unify data, break down silos, and act on real-time insights. Today, 1,900+ enterprises and 60% of the Fortune 100 rely on Sprinklr to help them deliver consistent, trusted customer experiences worldwide. **Job Description** ------------------- **What You’ll Do** * Conduct manual and automated accessibility testing on web/mobile applications using tools such as Axe, * Lighthouse, NVDA, VoiceOver, JAWS, TalkBack, and others. * Validate conformance against WCAG 2.1/2.2, Section 508, European Accessibility Act, and other relevant standards. * Partner with designers, developers, and product managers to identify accessibility issues early and advocate for inclusive design. * Document accessibility bugs clearly with reproducible steps, severity levels, and remediation recommendations. * Participate in VPAT and Accessibility Conformance Report (ACR) preparation for enterprise and public- sector clients. * Drive accessibility awareness through training, reviews, and process improvements across the engineering team. * Assist in integrating accessibility checks into the CI/CD pipeline and test automation suite. * Stay updated on evolving accessibility regulations, assistive technologies, and emerging trends. **What Makes You Qualified** * In-depth understanding of WCAG guidelines, ARIA roles, keyboard navigation, and semantic HTML. * Proficiency with screen readers (e.g., NVDA, VoiceOver), contrast checkers, and a11y automation tools (e.g., Axe-core, Pa11y, etc.). * Familiarity with development technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and the impact of code on accessibility. * Experience creating and maintaining accessibility defect reports and working with dev teams on remediation. * Excellent communication skills and the ability to advocate accessibility to technical and non-technical stakeholders. * Familiarity with Agile methodologies and Jira/Zephyr for test management. * IAAP Certified Professional in Accessibility Core Competencies (CPACC) or Web Accessibility Specialist (WAS) is a plus * Experience with automated test frameworks integrating accessibility checks (e.g., Cypress + Axe, Selenium + Axe-core) is a plus. **We focus on our mission**: Sprinklr was founded in 2009 to solve a big problem: growing enterprise complexity that separated brands from their customers. Our vision was clear: to unify fragmented teams, tools and data — helping large organizations build deeper, more meaningful connections with the people they serve. Today, Sprinklr has a unified, AI-native platform for four product suites: Sprinklr Service, Sprinklr Social, Sprinklr Marketing, and Sprinklr Insights. Sprinklr is here to do three things: * Lead a new category of enterprise software that we call Unified-CXM. * Empower companies to deliver next generation, unified engagement journeys that reimagine the customer experience. * Create a culture of customer obsession, with trust, teamwork, and accountability. **We believe in our product**: Customers who value exceptional customer experiences have what they need on our single unified platform, built with an operating system approach on a single codebase. That means that everything — and everyone — can work together to service, respond, sell, and market to customers on the channels they prefer. While Unified Customer Experience Management (Unified-CXM) as a category is just getting started, we are well on our way to creating a no-compromise, unified approach to better customer experiences for the world’s leading enterprise brands. **We invest in our people**: We offer a comprehensive suite of benefits designed to help each member of our team thrive. Sprinklr believes that you should be able to get the type of care you need for your personal well-being when you need it. We offer you and your family voluntary healthcare coverage in countries where applicable. We believe it is important to take time off – it is essential for your mental and physical wellbeing. We provide Sprinklrites with paid time off to recharge and spend time with loved ones. We want to grow our talent with purpose. Our open Mentoring Program is designed to create meaningful connections that support growth and amplify our focus. To learn more about employee benefits by region, [click here](https://sprinklrbenefits.com/). To learn more about all-things-Sprinklr, visit our candidate re

jobs.title: Senior Accessibility Analyst – Section 508
jobs.description: Are you ready for your next challenge? Leidos Digital Modernization team is looking for a **Senior Accessibility Analyst**, who will serve as a subject matter expert to support the provisioning of enterprise-wide guidance, policies, evaluation, and oversight for the accessibility of DoD information for disabled individuals, known as DoD Section 508. You will also provide analytical and programmatic support services to increase the awareness of Section 508. The successful candidate will be a proven performer and self-starter with the ability to work independently as well as part of a team. **This position is 50% hybrid, however you must be within commuting distance of The Mark Center to go in office two to three days per week.** **This position requires U.S. citizenship and the ability to obtain and maintain a Secret clearance.** **What will you do in this role?** * This position will support the DoD Section 508 provision of enterprise-wide guidance, policies, and standards. * This job is intended to provide analytical and programmatic support services to increase the awareness, application and effectiveness of Section 508. * The successful candidate will be a proven performer and self-starter with the ability to work independently as well as part of a team. * Apply Section 508 of the American with Disabilities Act (ADA) knowledge and understanding to support DoD CIO strategic goals in this area. * Evaluate the Department’s level of compliance for creating Section 508 compliant Electronic & Information Technology (EIT). * Provide recommendations on how to improve the DOD CIOs efforts to develop, procure, and maintain EIT solutions that increase the accessibility of the DOD EIT environment. * Recommend considerations to be incorporated into the planning and formulation of Section 508 policyProvide knowledge regarding Department-wide policy guidance and oversight for federally mandated Section 508 implementation. * Evaluate the Department’s level of compliance for creating Section 508-compliant Electronic & Information Technology (EIT). * Provide recommendations on how to improve the DOD CIO's efforts to develop, procure, and maintain solutions that increase the accessibility of the DOD EIT environment. **Basic Qualifications:** * Master's degree and at least 12+ years of prior relevant experience in Section 508 capacity, additional years of experience will be accepted in lieu of a degree. * Deep understanding of Section 508 standards, WCAG 2.0 guidelines, and related accessibility regulations. * Knowledge of commonly used Assistive Technologies and electronic document accessibility practices. * Good oral and written communication skills * Experience formulating policy, guidelines, and training materials. * Proficiency in both manual and automated accessibility testing methods and tools (e.g., ANDI, MS Accessibility Insights, Color Contrast Analyzer, Axe, DevTools). * Proficient in using office 365 Applications and has a deep understanding of formatting and the use of built-in tools when authoring source documents. * Familiarity with Content Management Systems (CMS) and basic understanding of HTML/CSS for accessibility improvements. * Solid knowledge of SharePoint and collaboration tools. * Solid presentation skills and development of presentation charts. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. * U.S. citizenship with and ability to obtain a Secret security clearance. If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares. **Original Posting:** --------------------- February 11, 2026 For U.S. Positions: While subject to change based on business needs, Leidos reasonably anticipates that this job requisition will remain open for at least 3 days with an anticipated close date of no earlier than 3 days after the original posting date as listed above. **Pay Range:** -------------- Pay Range $92,300.00 - $166,850.00 The Leidos pay range for this job level is a general guideline only and not a guarantee of compensation or salary. Additional factors considered in extending an offer include (but are not limited to) responsibilities of the job, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity, alignment with market data, applicable bargaining agreement (if any), or other law. URL: https://leidos.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/External/job/Senior-Accessibility-Analyst---Section-508_R-00175868

jobs.title: Product Manager, Accessibility
jobs.description: At Appspace, we’re passionate about creating better work experiences for people everywhere, and we’re looking for people that feel the same way. Our global office locations and flexible work culture help you work wherever and however you’re at your best. Plus, we take the time to help you enjoy your work, build lasting connections, and grow your role. Join the Appspace team and be a part of a culture that’s helping people everywhere love where they work. Your Role as a Product Manager, Accessibility: You will develop and lead accessibility strategy and execution across the Appspace platform. This is a hands-on technical role within Product Management that requires both deep accessibility expertise and the ability to work directly with our largest global customers. You'll define our accessibility roadmap, make prioritization decisions, and work as a trusted advisor to customers for whom accessibility is mission-critical. This means you need to credibly explain WCAG requirements, respond to complex compliance questions, and sometimes push back on unrealistic demands – all while maintaining strong customer relationships. You'll partner closely with design, engineering, product managers, and QA to integrate accessibility throughout our development process. You'll also work with Sales and Customer Success on customer engagements, RFP responses, and roadmap presentations. A Day in the Life of a Product Manager, Accessibility: Customer & Strategy * Serve as the primary accessibility contact for our largest customers, handling executive-level conversations about compliance requirements, roadmap plans, and barrier remediation * Develop and prioritize the accessibility roadmap in alignment with WCAG 2.1/2.2 Level AA and European Accessibility Act (EAA) requirements * Partner with Sales to respond to accessibility requirements in RFPs and customer negotiations Execution & Delivery * Translate compliance requirements and customer needs into clear user stories and acceptance criteria for development teams * Triage accessibility issues reported by users and customers, prioritize remediation, and track progress * Manage third-party accessibility audits and maintain accurate VPAT/ACR documentation * Define and track metrics on accessibility performance (defect trends, audit findings, escalations) Cross-functional Leadership * Act as the subject matter expert on accessibility for product managers, designers, and engineers * Work with design teams early in the process to ensure accessibility is built in from the start * Lead workshops and provide training on WCAG guidelines and inclusive design principles * Develop resources and workflows that make accessibility easier to integrate across the product lifecycle What You'll Need: Required: * 5+ years guiding product, design, and engineering teams on implementing WCAG 2.1/2.2 standards * Proven experience in customer-facing roles, including executive-level interactions about technical accessibility topics * Track record managing accessibility in a SaaS or complex software environment * Ability to balance compliance requirements with user experience and business needs * Strong communication skills – you can explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders Highly Desirable: * IAAP certification (CPACC, WAS, or CPWA) * Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies * Familiarity with EN 301 549 and Section 508 standards * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business, or related field The Perks of Working for Appspace: For all our team members, we offer a variety of benefits from competitive salaries, employer paid medical, dental and vision coverage and mental health resources. Additional perks include: * Flexible work schedules * Remote work opportunities * A casual dress work environment * Appspace Quiet Fridays (No non-essential internal meetings scheduled) * Training allowance * Training days off Disclaimer: Appspace is committed to equitable compensation practices and complies with all applicable local, state, and federal regulations. For jurisdictions that require pay scale disclosure, a general compensation range may be provided during the initial stages of the interview process. Final compensation will be based on multiple factors including experience, skills, certifications, and overall fit for the role. If you are located in a jurisdiction with specific pay transparency requirements, we will be happy to discuss the relevant range during your application process.

jobs.title: Lead Accessibility Consultant
jobs.description: The pay range is $115,000.00 - $206,000.00 Pay is based on several factors which vary based on position. These include labor markets and in some instances may include education, work experience and certifications. In addition to your pay, Target cares about and invests in you as a team member, so that you can take care of yourself and your family. Target offers eligible team members and their dependents comprehensive health benefits and programs, which may include medical, vision, dental, life insurance and more, to help you and your family take care of your whole selves. Other benefits for eligible team members include 401(k), employee discount, short term disability, long term disability, paid sick leave, paid national holidays, and paid vacation. Find competitive benefits from financial and education to well-being and beyond at https://corporate.target.com/careers/benefits. About Us: Working at Target means helping all families discover the joy of everyday life. We bring that vision to life through our values and culture. Learn more about Target here https://corporate.target.com/about. Target UX is a group of design, research, and accessibility practitioners and experts, with a passion for improving and innovating our digital experiences for Target’s guests, team members, and partners. Here, we pride ourselves on designing tangible, inspiring, and impactful solutions that serve all aspects of our business – and being a part of the team means you will play a critical role for Target’s digital experience by increasing ease, simplicity, value, and delight for every touchpoint. About the job: As a Lead Accessibility Consultant on the Digital Accessibility team, you’ll work alongside teams of developers, designers and product owners that are responsible for creating world-class accessible experiences for Target Guests and Target Team Members. This includes the creation of entirely new experiences, as well as improvements to existing ones. You’ll be well-positioned to make an impact as a Lead Accessibility Consultant working on friendly and high-performing teams operating at the heart of Target’s growing digital business. You’ll drive our team strategies and help mentor and up-skill our own team’s capabilities. In this role, you will be involved in the full product development cycle, providing accessibility consultation during the ideation, design, development and testing phases, providing final sign off prior to the experience being launched. You’ll use your WCAG knowledge to guide your partners in the design and development of innovative and accessible digital experiences. You’ll do this by experimenting and collaborating, through product discovery and design sprints, with both next-generation ideas and optimizations of current experiences in support of Target’s business strategies. You’ll bring empathy and guest insights to every business problem. You’ll participate in guest research and leverage data to inform design decisions. You’ll help to define objectives and results with product, design, engineering and business partners. You’ll push work forward with high standards and an approach that follows the voice of the guest. You’ll collaborate with a multi-disciplinary team of leaders, product designers, content designers, engineers, product managers, data analysts and researchers. You’ll constructively challenge current thinking across the enterprise, pushing boundaries and driving change that will deliver exceptional user-centric accessible experiences at scale. You’re a storyteller by nature, and you thrive on building relationships. You’re a proactive agent of change with a bias toward action. You’re skilled at influencing partners and working in a matrixed environment to develop and improve organization level processes. You’ll be the master of your own destiny in an environment that has strong support for work-life balance. We’ll support you along the way with robust onboarding, as well as personal and career development, to help you grow as a Lead Accessibility Consultant. Job duties may change at any time due to business needs. About you: Technical Experience * Expert skills in understanding WCAG 2.2 AA and working in an agile environment with developers and designers in applying requirements to create accessible experiences. * Proven ability to successfully lead accessibility efforts, throughout all phases of development, within large-scale projects and initiatives involving multiple cross-functional teams. * Experience developing procedures to guide consistent accessibility consulting across an organization. * Experience performing accessibility testing of web and app experiences with assistive technology, including keyboards and screen readers. Soft Skills * Able to work well in a large organization with many partners, with a demonstrated track record of building deep relationships and influencing peers and leaders. * Comfort operating with flexibility in your approach in order to deliver results

jobs.title: Instructional Designer
jobs.description: ****About team / role**** At WEX, we are on a mission to empower our employees through impactful, tailored learning experiences that drive growth and performance across the globe. As a member of our Learning Design and Development team, you will join a collaborative, forward-thinking group that embraces creativity, technology, and innovation in learning design. Our expanded portfolio is central to WEX's success, encompassing enterprise learning, leadership development, career management, and role-specific training aligned with critical business priorities. We are seeking a creative, detail-oriented Instructional Designer to bring these initiatives to life through engaging content and modern delivery methods. In this role, you will apply instructional design best practices to analyze needs, architect holistic learning solutions, and develop high-quality multimedia assets and interactive e-learning. By partnering closely with fellow IDs, HR and Learning Business Partners, key stakeholders, and SMEs, you will translate complex information into digestible, high-impact experiences for our diverse global audience. ****How you'll make an impact**** Instructional Design & Content Development * Conduct needs assessments and discovery sessions to define clear learning objectives. * Apply instructional design models and adult learning principles to craft effective learning experiences. * Create instructional materials, including course outlines, learning objectives, facilitator guides, job aids, and interactive training activities. * Collaborate with SMEs to source, validate, and refine content for accuracy and clarity. * Provide consultative guidance to stakeholders on learning strategies, design decisions, and content effectiveness throughout the development process. E-Learning & Multimedia Production * Develop project plans, storyboards, and wireframes for e-learning modules and training videos. * Design multimedia assets, including video, graphics, animations, and AI-narrated audio, using tools like Camtasia, Adobe Creative Cloud, Google Vids, and WellSaid Labs. * Leverage generative AI tools (e.g., Gemini, NotebookLM, ChatGPT) for brainstorming, content drafting, and refinement. * Build and publish interactive e-learning modules using authoring tools such as Articulate Storyline 360 and Rise 360. Project Management & Stakeholder Consultation * Design learning initiatives from concept through delivery, collaborating with business stakeholders to define objectives, manage timelines, and ensure solutions meet organizational needs and quality standards. * Provide guidance and recommendations to stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle, ensuring training is effective and aligns with business priorities. * Assist with facilitation of workshops or training sessions as needed. * Manage multiple projects simultaneously, meeting deadlines and maintaining high-quality standards. * Lead content review cycles, gather feedback, and manage approvals. * Provide exceptional service and communication throughout the development lifecycle. * Use Google Workspace, Jira, and other project management tools to document, track, and collaborate effectively. ****Experience you'll bring**** * Bachelor's degree, preferably in Instructional Design, Educational Technology, Education, Multimedia Production, Graphic Design, or a related field. * 2+ years of experience in e-learning development, multimedia production, and instructional design. * A portfolio showcasing your instructional design work and e-learning samples is strongly preferred. * Demonstrated Proficiency with: * Video Production: Experience with video recording and production via video conferencing (e.g., Zoom, Google Meet). * Video Editing & Production Software: Google Vids, Camtasia, Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects. * AI Voice Generation: WellSaid Labs or Google Vids (or similar text-to-speech tools). * E-Learning Authoring Tools: Articulate Storyline 360 and Rise 360. * Generative AI Tools: Gemini, NotebookLM, ChatGPT (or similar). * Graphic Design Software: Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign. * Google Workspace: Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive. * Strong Foundation in: * Instructional Design Principles and Adult Learning Theories. * User Experience (UX) design for digital learning environments. * Knowledge of web and video accessibility standards (e.g., WCAG, Section 508 compliance). * Strong project management skills with the ability to prioritize and execute tasks efficiently. * Outstanding customer service skills and a collaborative team player mindset. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. ****To Apply**** Please submit your resume, a cover letter, and a link to your portfolio (if available) showcasing relevant design samples, training videos, and e-learning projects. jobs.title: Senior Accessibility Manager
jobs.description: **About Us**At CBA our goal is to deliver dignity by design. At the forefront of this mission is our digital accessibility team, supporting our wider digital team to deliver digital platforms are inclusive and accessible to all. The digital accessibility team is part of the Retail Banking Services (RBS) Strategy Centre of Excellence (CoE) and sits within the award-winning Experience Design Chapter. **About The Role**As a Senior Accessibility Manager, you will report to the Digital Accessibility Chapter Lead and join a dedicated team of digital accessibility specialists. This 6-month fixed term role is pivotal to enhancing the accessibility of our digital platforms, primarily our internet banking website, NetBank. You'll work closely with product designers, product owners, and engineers to ensure our web interfaces are seamless and accessible. **What will your day look like?** * Work with designers to review Figma designs through an accessibility lens * Define accessibility build requirements for web engineers to apply accessibility semantics, properties and behaviours in code * Perform Web Content Accessibility Guidelines v2.2 level AA conformance testing and assistive technology review of new and updated web pages and provide accessibility sign off * Collaborate with the rest of the digital accessibility team to ensure our advice and approach is correct and consistent **What will you bring?** * You will bring significant experience in specialist digital accessibility roles working directly with design, technical and business roles * Extensive experience using assistive technologies, settings and adaptive strategies across PC, Mac, iPhone and Android devices * Deep knowledge of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) v2.2 and WAI-ARIA markup * Experience using accessibility testing tools * A knack for explaining digital accessibility to any audience in plain language * The ability to diagnose code-level web accessibility issues and give detailed recommendations (HTML & ARIA required, React preferred but not required) * Confidence and ability to work independently and autonomously * Proactive, pragmatic approach and a clear, adaptable communication style * Experience working in large and complex organisations (preferred but not required) * Experience working with web component design systems (preferred but not required) If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career. We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696. Advertising End Date: 26/02/2026

jobs.title: Quality Assurance Specialist (Vacancy# VAR003262)
jobs.description: The incumbent performs inspections on products for delivery of the braille and talking book programs to validate compliance with requirements for accessibility, usability and functionality. Performs assessments of product test results. Provides analysis, recommendations, and reports to the product manager as to the acceptability of a product for use in the braille and talking book programs. Plans, develops, organizes, and administers test procedures for product quality assurance. Performs product acceptance inspections at all phases of the product life cycle including prototype/proof-of-concept, first articles production, and operations/maintenance. Initiates or recommends corrective action for quality issues/problems and product defects. Investigates, validates and confirms issues, problems or defects reported to the product manager by other product testing resources external to the organization. Negotiates and coordinates complex issues pertaining to the hardware and/or software functionality of products for delivery of the braille and talking book programs. Reviews technical artifacts, requirements and specifications. Prepares inspection reports that include such information as requirements traceability matrix, type of deficiencies, requirements that are out of compliance, detailed steps to reproduce issues, trends (favorable and unfavorable), etc. Is responsible for the life cycle quality of products and services for delivery of the braille and talking book program through incorporation of verification and validation practices into the product development process.

jobs.title: OPS Content Accessibility Support Specialist
jobs.description: **Job Summary** The OPS Content Accessibility Support Specialist assists with the creation, remediation, and evaluation of electronic documents and multimedia to ensure compliance with accessibility standards, including WCAG 2.1, and ADA Title II. This position supports faculty and staff to help make instructional materials ADA Title II compliant by reviewing course content and providing consultation, training and hands-on remediation services and assistance, while applying and learning best practices in digital accessibility. **Job Description** **Typical duties include, but not limited to:** * Review online course materials to check for accessibility and usability issues. * Remediate documents and multimedia to meet accessibility standards. * Assist faculty and staff with making documents, presentations, and videos ADA Title II compliant (for example, adding alt text, headings, or captions; using MathML or Word equations in the conversion of handwritten lecture notes). * Support the instructional design team by updating or remediating course content in Canvas or other platforms. * Help organize and assist with file remediation workshops and webinars for faculty and staff. * Maintain documentation and tracking of remediation requests and completed work. * Contribute to the creation of easy-to-follow training guides and resources on accessibility topics. * Learn about accessibility standards (such as WCAG and Section 508) and new tools to improve course design. * Suggest simple, practical ways to make learning materials more user-friendly and accessible. **Other duties:** * Perform other job-related duties as assigned. **Additional Job Description** **Required Qualifications:** * High school diploma or equivalent. * Basic computer skills and familiarity with common software * Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn new tools and processes * Experience in digital accessibility and/or document remediation * Ability to explain accessibility practices clearly **Preferred Qualifications:** * Knowledge of WCAG 2.1 Level AA, ADA Title II, and Section 508. * Experience assisting with online courses or educational materials. * Familiarity with accessibility features in Word, PowerPoint, or Adobe Acrobat.Familiarity with STEM specific accessibility technology (for example, MathML or LaTeX) **Knowledge, Skills, & Abilities:** * Basic knowledge of computers, common software applications, and online content (e.g., Microsoft Office, Canvas, or other learning platforms). * Familiarity with accessibility evaluation tools and assistive technologies (for example, Microsoft Accessibility Checker, Adobe Acrobat Pro, etc,). * Knowledge of the purpose of digital accessibility and an interest in learning accessibility standards (e.g., WCAG 2.1 Level AA, Section 508). * Strong attention to detail when reviewing documents, videos, and online content. * Good written and verbal communication skills and an ability to communicate with faculty, staff, and students in a clear and appropriate manner. * Ability to adapt to new tools, accessibility practices, or instructional technologies as they evolve. * Ability to follow established guidelines and procedures for reviewing and updating course materials. _FGCU is a State University System of Florida member and an Equal Opportunity and Equal Access employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status._

jobs.title: Manager, Central Quality Accessibility Engineering - Apple Services Engineering
jobs.description: In this role you will be responsible for leading a team of Quality Engineers who own the test planning, test writing, test execution, and result reporting for critical software features for upcoming software releases specific to Apple media products and services. You will be responsible for representing the customer experience for customers who require accommodations to interact with and to use Apple products. To accomplish this, you will work directly with our project managers, cross functional partners, developers, and engineering teams to review upcoming projects, offer feedback and suggestions for accessibility improvements, and represent accessibility concerns in broader engineering discussions and bug reviews. Your passion, experience, and advocacy can change and shape the future of our products for everyone! Minimum Qualifications * Over 5 year’s successfully leading teams in Quality Assurance Engineering delivering software and services through the entire project lifecycle. * Over 5 years in quality assurance engineering, SDET, Engineering Project Management, or related roles * Versatile problem solver with a strong analytical mindset, adept at tackling challenges in complex environments through creative and critical thinking. * Skilled in deconstructing problems, resolving conflicts, and leading teams to innovative solutions. * Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to lead and influence cross-functional teams and effectively communicate with executive leadership. * A familiarity with screen readers like VoiceOver, TalkBack, NVDA and Narrator * LANGUAGES: Swift, SwiftUI, Python, unit testing, git, Bash/Zsh * Experience with test automation frameworks and UI automation * Experience with manual end-to-end software integrity testing * Experience testing client UI * Passionate about equitable UI /UX for everyone * A comfortable understanding of WCAG guidelines and ARIA for accessible web experiences * Bachelor's Degree or equivalent industry experience Preferred Qualifications * Execution-oriented leadership with strong attention to detail * Ability to balance technical depth with business understanding * Problem-solving mindset with a bias toward automation and scalability * Track record of hiring and developing engineering talent * Experience working on products with a global footprint and diverse customer needs Pay & Benefits At Apple, base pay is one part of our total compensation package and is determined within a range. This provides the opportunity to progress as you grow and develop within a role. The base pay range for this role is between $198,300 and $342,800, and your base pay will depend on your skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Apple employees also have the opportunity to become an Apple shareholder through participation in Apple’s discretionary employee stock programs. Apple employees are eligible for discretionary restricted stock unit awards, and can purchase Apple stock at a discount if voluntarily participating in Apple’s Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You’ll also receive benefits including: Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits, a range of discounted products and free services, and for formal education related to advancing your career at Apple, reimbursement for certain educational expenses — including tuition. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary bonuses or commission payments as well as relocation. Learn more about Apple Benefits. Note: Apple benefit, compensation and employee stock programs are subject to eligibility requirements and other terms of the applicable plan or program. Apple is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity. We seek to promote equal opportunity for all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

jobs.title: Website Specialist
jobs.description: **You’ve discovered something special. A company that cares. Cares about leading the way in construction, engineering, manufacturing and renewable energy. Cares about redefining how energy is designed, applied and consumed. Cares about thoughtfully growing to meet market demands. And ─ as “one of the Healthiest 100 Workplaces in America” ─ is focused on the mind/body/soul of team members through our**[**Culture of Care**](https://www.faithtechinc.com/culture/)**.** The Website Specialist is responsible for managing day-to-day website operations, supporting content updates, improving site performance and ensuring a consistent, accessible digital experience across channels. They will partner closely with marketing communications, partners and Business Technology to execute updates, optimize pages for SEO/UX and maintain a scalable governance model. **MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS** Education: Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Digital Media or a related field Experience: 2 years of website management experience. * Expertise with website platforms (i.e. WordPress, Storyblok, Squarespace), component-based design systems, and reusable content blocks. * Working knowledge of HTML/CSS for content formatting and light fixes. * Experience with GA4 (basic reporting, event tracking) and on-page SEO. * Understanding of accessibility principles (WCAG 2.2) and inclusive content practices. * Understanding of SEO tools and site performance tools. * Proficiency with Active Campaign, Sprout Social, project management tools or similar marketing platforms. * Creative thinker and problem solver with a passion for digital marketing. * Strong organizational and project management skills. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Travel: 5-10% Work Schedule: This position works between the hours of 7 am and 5 pm, Monday through Friday. Ability to work a flexible schedule is necessary. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** **Website Operations & Content**: Build, edit and publish pages, components and assets in content management systems. * Maintains site structure, navigation, redirects and internal linking hygiene. * Executes content updates for campaigns, product launches and press releases with high attention to detail. * Manages digital asset organization (naming, versions, alt text, tagging). **Optimization & Quality**: Apply on-page SEO best practices (meta titles/descriptions, page copy, schema basics, internal links, etc.). * Conducts regular QA (broken links, 404s, rendering issues, forms, mobile responsiveness). * Ensures accessibility compliance to WCAG 2.2 AA standards (alt text, color contrast, focus order, keyboard navigation). * Focuses on user experience (UX) to improve readability, scanability and engagement. **Governance & Process**: Maintain content calendars, workflows, templates and documentation. * Triages and delivers web requests via the project management request board. * Supports localization and translation workflows; coordinates with internal or vendor translators, as needed. * Assists with basic testing of platform updates/releases and coordinate fixes. **Collaboration** * Partners with Marketing on campaign landing pages and A/B tests. * Works with web partner and/or Business Technology on technical issues, integrations and environments. * Coordinates with Legal/Compliance for policy and regulatory content updates (privacy, cookies, terms). * Provides back up support for social media, email and analytics operations, including scheduling posts, preparing basic content updates, assisting with email sends, executing routine publishing tasks to ensure continuity during high-volume periods or team absences, track and report on platform performance, set up data tracking and provide thoughtful recommendations based on analytics collected. * Performs other related duties as required and assigned. The job description and responsibilities described are intended to provide guidelines for job expectations and the employee's ability to perform the position described. It is not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all functions, responsibilities, skills, and abilities. Additional functions and requirements may be assigned by supervisors as deemed appropriate. **How Does FTI Give YOU the Chance to Thrive?** If you’re energized by new challenges, FTI provides you with many opportunities. Joining FTI opens doors to redefine what’s possible for your future. Once you’re a team member, you’re supported and provided with the knowledge and resources to [achieve your career goals](https://www.faithtechinc.com/careers) with FTI. jobs.title: Web Accessibility Coordinator
jobs.description: Start your career in public service – JOIN OUR TEAM Georgia Technology Authority (GTA) a Great Place to Work® certified! The Georgia Technology Authority (GTA) is currently seeking a Web Accessibility Coordinator in the Digital Services & Solutions Department. The Georgia Technology Authority (GTA) currently manages the delivery of IT infrastructure services to 89 Executive Branch agencies and managed network services to more than 1,200 state and local government entities. IT infrastructure services encompass mainframes, servers, service desk, end user computing, disaster recovery and security. Managed network services include the state’s wide and local area networks, voice, cable and wiring, and conferencing services. Want to know more about our AWARD-WINNING Authority visit: https://gta.georgia.gov/ JOB SUMMARY: The State Web Accessibility Coordinator ensures that all digital services, websites, applications, and electronic communications provided by state agencies are accessible to all residents, including individuals with disabilities. This position serves as the state's subject matter expert on digital accessibility, responsible for developing and guiding compliance strategies, training programs, and policy implementation in alignment with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act, the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), and Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 AA. - Lead the development and implementation of statewide web accessibility standards, policies, and best practices. - Conduct regular accessibility audits of public-facing state websites and digital services using automated tools and manual testing. - Provide expert consultation to state agencies on digital accessibility compliance, including procurement reviews and remediation strategies. - Coordinate and deliver training and capacity-building programs for agency web and content teams. - Collaborate with legal, IT, communications, and disability advocacy groups to promote inclusive digital experiences. - Monitor legislative and regulatory developments related to digital accessibility and ensure timely updates to policies and practices. - Develop reporting mechanisms and metrics to track accessibility progress across agencies. - Serve as the primary liaison to federal agencies, accessibility vendors, and community stakeholders on digital inclusion efforts. - Perform other duties as assigned. CORE Competencies: - Empathetic Leadership & Emotional Intelligence – Demonstrates the ability to lead with empathy, understand diverse perspectives, and foster inclusive, trust-based working relationships. - Executive Presentation & Facilitation – Effectively communicates complex concepts through clear, engaging presentations tailored to diverse stakeholder audiences. - Analytical Research & Insight Generation – Conducts thorough research, synthesizes information, and applies insights to inform decisions, strategy, and continuous improvement. - Strategic Thinking & Business Acumen – Anticipates trends, evaluates risks and opportunities, and aligns initiatives with organizational goals and long-term priorities. - Advanced Written & Verbal Communication – Communicates with clarity, precision, and professionalism across all levels of the organization. - Collaborative Team Effectiveness – Builds strong partnerships, contributes to high-performing teams, and promotes shared accountability for outcomes. - Cross-Functional Influence & Stakeholder Management – Successfully influences and aligns cross-functional teams and stakeholders to achieve shared objectives without direct authority. AGENCY SPECIFIC QUALIFICATIONS: Minimum Qualifications: - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Human-Computer Interaction, Information Technology, Public Administration, or a related field AND Three (3) years of experience in web accessibility, digital compliance, or related roles WITH: o Deep knowledge of WCAG, Section 508, ADA Title II, and digital accessibility testing tools (e.g., Axe, WAVE, Siteimprove, JAWS/NVDA/VoiceOver). o Experience with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and common content management systems (e.g., Drupal, WordPress). o Strong communication and training skills with the ability to explain technical topics to non-technical audiences. o Demonstrated ability to manage cross-agency projects and influence policy adoption. Note: An equivalent combination of education and job-specific experience that provided the knowledge, experience, and competencies required to successfully perform the job at the level listed may be substituted on a year-over-year basis. Preferred Qualifications: - IAAP certifications (e.g., CPACC, WAS, CPWA). - Experience working in a government or large public-sector organization. - Familiarity with inclusive design principles and user research involving people with disabilities. COMPENSATION/WORKER TYPE/ADDITIONAL DETAILS: Office Location: 47 Trinity Ave, Atlanta GA Worker Type: Hybrid – (in-office: 2-3 days per week) Target Hiring Salar

jobs.title: Senior Accessibility Manager
jobs.description: * **You are a passionate digital accessibility specialist** * **You are committed to providing the best digital experience for all customers** * **Together we deliver digital innovation that enhances our customers’ financial wellbeing** **About us** At CBA our goal is to deliver dignity by design. At the forefront of this mission is our digital accessibility team, supporting our wider digital team to deliver digital platforms are inclusive and accessible to all. The digital accessibility team is part of the Retail Banking Services (RBS) Strategy Centre of Excellence (CoE) and sits within the award-winning Experience Design Chapter. **About the role** As a Senior Accessibility Manager, you will report to the Digital Accessibility Chapter Lead and join a dedicated team of digital accessibility specialists. This 6-month fixed term role is pivotal to enhancing the accessibility of our digital platforms, primarily our internet banking website, NetBank. You'll work closely with product designers, product owners, and engineers to ensure our web interfaces are seamless and accessible. **What will your day look like?** * Work with designers to review Figma designs through an accessibility lens * Define accessibility build requirements for web engineers to apply accessibility semantics, properties and behaviours in code * Perform Web Content Accessibility Guidelines v2.2 level AA conformance testing and assistive technology review of new and updated web pages and provide accessibility sign off * Collaborate with the rest of the digital accessibility team to ensure our advice and approach is correct and consistent **What will you bring?** * You will bring significant experience in specialist digital accessibility roles working directly with design, technical and business roles * Extensive experience using assistive technologies, settings and adaptive strategies across PC, Mac, iPhone and Android devices * Deep knowledge of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) v2.2 and WAI-ARIA markup * Experience using accessibility testing tools * A knack for explaining digital accessibility to any audience in plain language * The ability to diagnose code-level web accessibility issues and give detailed recommendations (HTML & ARIA required, React preferred but not required) * Confidence and ability to work independently and autonomously * Proactive, pragmatic approach and a clear, adaptable communication style * Experience working in large and complex organisations (preferred but not required) * Experience working with web component design systems (preferred but not required) If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career. We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696. Advertising End Date: 26/02/2026
