jobs.title: Senior Visual / Product Designer
jobs.description: CapTech is an award-winning consulting firm that collaborates with clients to achieve what’s possible through the power of technology. At CapTech, we’re passionate about the work we do and the results we achieve for our clients. From the outset, our founders shared a collective passion to create a consultancy centered on strong relationships that would stand the test of time. Today we work alongside clients that include Fortune 100 companies, mid-sized enterprises, and government agencies, a list that spans across the country. CapTech’s Customer Experience (CX) team seeks a Senior Visual Designer / Product Designer with a track record of designing sophisticated user experience and digital brand experiences that are visually striking, brand-forward, and accessible. Our designers are passionate problem-solvers who bring both creativity and craft to complex business challenges. They elevate the visual quality of our products and services, keeping pace with evolving design methods, tools, and technologies. **This could be the position for you if:** · You are a visual storyteller with a portfolio of modern, polished, and engaging UI and brand design work across web and mobile platforms. · You excel at designing interfaces, components, and layouts that balance beauty and usability. · You can create everything from product UIs and prototypes to icons, brand graphics, and illustrations. · You’re comfortable working within design systems and know when to push their evolution with fresh, creative thinking. · You enjoy collaborating with researchers, developers, analysts, and other designers to create cohesive and impactful experiences. · You are a self-starter and are able to meet tight-deadlines, while being able to incorporate feedback from clients and Creative Directors, all while collaborating closely with project teams. **Responsibilities may include:** · Designing engaging digital experiences for web and mobile products, as well as extending and enhancing existing designs based on established design systems. · Producing clean, informative, and concise presentations to clients and internal teams that communicate the rationale behind your designs and recommendations. · Presenting design concepts and rationale to clients and internal teams in a clear and compelling manner. · Conducting competitive brand or experience analyses and delivering innovative solutions and recommendations. · Consulting with clients and team members about the benefits of accessible design. · Contributing to and supporting the creation of personas, customer journeys, and prototypes that follow interaction design and usability best practices. · Incorporating user research, customer insights, and usability testing results to inform design decisions. · Collaborating with developers to ensure design fidelity and accessibility. · Researching and understanding design and technology trends and helping determine their relevance for our recommended solutions. · Exploring and leveraging AI-powered design tools and workflows, staying current with emerging technologies, and experimenting with new approaches to enhance creativity, efficiency, and impact. · 3-5 years of professional experience, ideally in a consulting or agency context. · 4-year+ degree in Design or a related field. · Strong written, verbal, conceptual, and visual communication skills. · Proficiency in designing developer-ready visual assets and UI components in Figma is required, with preference given to candidates who demonstrate expertise in organizing files for clarity, applying consistent styles and naming conventions, and utilizing advanced features such as auto layout, component libraries, variables, and design tokens to support scalable and efficient design handoff. · Proficiency with Illustrator, Photoshop, and InDesign, as well as PowerPoint. · Working familiarity with the role of Product Designer is a plus. · Familiarity with user research tools (e.g., Optimal Workshop, UserTesting, SurveyMonkey) is a plus. · Experience with video editing and motion design is a plus · Working knowledge of Agile product development lifecycles and practices is a plus. · Familiarity with WCAG and accessibility standards is a plus. **Please include a link to your online portfolio with your application in order to be considered.** We want everyone at CapTech to be able to envision a lasting and rewarding career here, which is why we offer a variety of career paths based on your skills and passions. You decide where and how you want to develop, and we help get you there with customizable career progression and a comprehensive benefits package to support you along the way.Alongside our suite of traditional benefits encompassing generous PTO, health coverage, disability insurance, paid family leave and more, we’ve launched extended benefits to help meet our employees’ needs. * Learning & Development – Programs offering certification and tuition support, digital on-demand learning courses, mentorship

jobs.title: Senior Advisor, Accessibility
jobs.description: Application Deadline: 02/12/2026 Address: 100 King Street West Job Family Group: Strategy & Change Manages Accessibility Topics Identifies key sustainability issues and advances management programs relating to the company’s Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) and Sustainability performance. Acts as a specialist resource for business stakeholders to ensure sustainability requirements are built into business processes. Develops and supports the enterprise sustainability strategy, stakeholder and employee engagement, and management reporting/disclosure. * This role directly supports the Enterprise Accessibility Office Strategy, Governance and Capacity Building mandate of the Enterprise Accessibility Office * Identifies and supports opportunities to drive inclusive and superior customer and employee experience through accessibility initiatives * Manages engagement and disclosures with external and internal stakeholders with a focus on consultation and continuous improvement * Build accessibility awareness, coordination, and capability across the enterprise​to support all components of accessibility at BMO * Fosters a culture aligned to BMO purpose, values and strategy and role models BMO values and behaviours in all that they do. * Ensures alignment between values and behaviour that fosters diversity and inclusion. * Regularly connects work to BMO’s purpose, sets inspirational goals, defines clear expected outcomes, and ensures clear accountability for follow through. * Builds interdependent teams that collaborate across functional and operating groups to create the highest value for all stakeholders. * Attracts, retains, and enables the career development of top talent. * Improves team performance, recognizes and rewards performance, coaches employees, supports their development, and manages poor performance. * Provides strategic input into business decisions as a trusted advisor. * Acts as a subject matter expert on relevant regulations and policies. * May jobs.title: Front End Web Developer
jobs.specific_location: Charlottesville, Virginia, United States Of America,UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION **Department Overview** ----------------------- The Office of University Communications at the University of Virginia is responsible for telling the remarkable and evolving story of UVA. Our team of writers, artists, strategists, communicators, and brand stewards proudly celebrate, promote, and publicize the University’s iconic reputation, accomplishments, brand, and history. University Communications works to inform and inspire audiences, ensuring the University’s impact is widely recognized across the UVA community, the Commonwealth of Virginia, and beyond. **Position Summary** -------------------- The Office of University Communications seeks a Front-End Developer who is passionate about merging the art of web design and programming to showcase UVA Today news stories, multimedia, and other marketing content across UVA websites and mass email platforms. In this role, the Front-End Developer will curate a range of written and visual news content daily and create beautiful, accessible, and often interactive web pages and emails that engage diverse audiences. This position is part of a four-person Digital Development and User Experience team and collaborates closely with a multidisciplinary group of professionals including news writers, editors, photographers, video producers, designers, and marketers. Together, this team supports University Communications’ mission to position UVA as a national model of excellence in public higher education. **Responsibilities** -------------------- ### **Web & Email Development** * Develop visually compelling and interactive web pages and daily emails for **UVA Today**, the University’s news and content platform. * Create and maintain interactive and multimedia content, including photo galleries, slide shows, animations, videos, and other embedded digital elements. * Play an integral role in the design and presentation of the UVA Today website and email communications. ### **Accessibility, Performance & Optimization** * Ensure all content meets digital accessibility standards and is responsive across multiple browsers and mobile devices. * Contribute to improvements in day-to-day website and email functionality, including search engine optimization (SEO) best practices. ### **Collaboration & Continuous Improvement** * Contribute to Digital Development and User Experience team initiatives, including website refresh projects and quality assurance testing across a range of University Communications websites. * Perform other tasks as assigned, including improvements to onsite search tools and calendar functionality. **Minimum Qualifications** -------------------------- * Bachelor’s degree in web design, web development, computer science, or a related discipline. * Minimum of one year of professional experience in a web development role; experience in a fast-paced, content-driven environment preferred (two or more years strongly preferred). * Experience managing websites and website design projects. * Strong attention to detail, ability to work independently, and capability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. * Demonstrated interest in continuous learning and skill development. * Ability to adapt to new digital tools, emerging technologies, and evolving digital design trends. ### **Required Technical Skills** * UX/UI design * HTML5 * HTML email development * CSS3 * JavaScript * Experience with a JavaScript library (e.g., jQuery or Tailwind) * SVG animations * Content Management Systems (CMS) * Adobe Creative Suite * Microsoft Office Suite * Testing and debugging * Responsive development **Preferred Qualifications** ---------------------------- * Experience managing mass email programs. * Knowledge of WCAG AA digital accessibility standards. * Experience applying SEO best practices. **Physical Demands** -------------------- This position is primarily sedentary and involves extensive use of desktop computers. Occasional travel may be required to attend meetings and University programs. **Salary Range** ---------------- Anticipated hiring range: **$70,000 – $80,000**, commensurate with education and experience. **Position Type & Work Location** --------------------------------- * Exempt-level, benefited position * This position will remain open until filled **About UVA and the Community** ------------------------------- The University of Virginia, founded by Thomas Jefferson in 1819, is a premier public institution of higher education located in Charlottesville, Virginia. UVA offers a rich academic environment, a strong sense of community, and access to the cultural, recreational, and natural amenities of Central Virginia. **Application Timeline** ------------------------ This position will remain open until filled. **Additional Requirements** --------------------------- * Background check required * This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship **How to Apply** ---------------- Please appl

jobs.title: Enterprise Content Management Administrator
jobs.description: PSR Associates is a consulting and talent solutions firm that connects qualified IT professionals with great opportunities. Whether you're looking for a contract or permanent position, we can help you find the right fit for your skills and experience. We have a team of experienced recruiters who know the IT industry inside and out, and we work with you every step of the way to ensure a smooth and successful transition. PSR Connecting Talent, Crafting Success. **Content Management/WCAG 2.1 Remediation Position Description** We are seeking an Enterprise Content Management Administrator to join our team. **The selected candidate will work a hybrid schedule consisting of two (2) days each week onsite in Lansing, MI and three (3) days each week remotely.** **Summary:** This position will provide Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 testing and remediation for Michigan Department of Civil Rights (MDCR) for Sitecore Content Management Solution site and content within the CMS. This individual must have Sitecore designer and author experience. Remediation of digital content includes PDF, Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and images. Key actions involve structuring documents, tagging content, adding alt text, and establishing logical reading orders and more. This position will work independently under MDCR direction and prioritization following e-Michigan web content standards. **Skillset:** * 3+ years of working experience with ADA remediation. * 3+ years of experience with Sitecore Content Management Solution. * In depth knowledge of WCAG 2.1 Guidelines. * Accessibility testing to ensure conformance to the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) requirements, specifically with WCAG 2.1 AA. * Experience with development and maintenance of web solutions using Sitecore CMS. * Ability to conform to the State of Michigan (SOM) Look & Feel Standards. * Familiarity with accessibility standards and best practices. * Strong background with ADA Remediation of digital content including * PDF * Microsoft Word * Excel * PowerPoint * Images * Reports on multiple formats * Key actions involve structuring documents, tagging content, adding alt text, and establishing logical reading orders. * Good communication both verbal and written. ***Please note that any false information on your resume or application could lead to the offer being withdrawn or even termination after hire.*** Apply This position is open. This job was posted on Sat Jan 31 2026. Minimum$77K /yr $85K /yr Average Maximum$94K /yr

jobs.title: Digital Transformation Project Manager
jobs.company: The National World War II Museum
jobs.description: The National WWII Museum is currently seeking a Digital Transformation Project Manager. The Digital Transformation Project Manager will work closely under the leadership of the Associate Vice President of Web Development and Digital Strategy to ensure the successful planning, execution, and evaluation of the Museum’s multifaceted digital transformation initiative. With a goal of becoming the most accessible source for trusted knowledge on the American experience in World War II, the Museum has launched an initiative to holistically transform its digital presence, bringing its wealth of WWII content to more people and reinforcing its role as a historical authority. This initiative includes development of a reimagined Museum website, a new and integrated digital collections experience, a centralized online content hub for K-12 educational resources, a modernized digital WWII encyclopedia as well as an improved online ticketing experience, technology and security upgrades, enhanced online giving functionality, and more. With nearly every Museum department involved in this digital transformation initiative, the Project Manager will help coordinate assignments and reviews across all stakeholders, ensuring positive progress toward our goals. Responsibilities will include participating in meetings with project vendors and relaying updates to colleagues; developing timelines and project plans; assigning tasks to colleagues; monitoring progress toward project milestones; facilitating review/approval processes; and helping ensure that project goals, budgets, and deadlines are met Requirements * Lead end-to-end management of digital transformation projects, including development of project plans, timelines, and budgets. * Serve as the primary liaison between internal teams (marketing, IT, curatorial, education, historians, etc.) and external vendors. * Gather project requirements and translate them into actionable tasks with deadlines for developers, designers, content creators, etc. * Proactively monitor the progress of projects, identify challenges and opportunities, and provide regular project status updates. * Provide quality assurance by ensuring all web content meets accessibility standards (WCAG), usability best practices, and museum branding guidelines. Conduct testing and troubleshooting before launch. * Collaborate with colleagues to monitor site performance metrics and make recommendations for improvements to the Museum’s digital presence. * Stay on top of digital trends and best practices to ensure Museum web products remain up to date and have a competitive advantage. * Undertake other projects as assigned by supervisor. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Web Development, Digital Media, Project Management, Marketing, or related field. * Minimum of three years of experience managing projects, preferably web-related projects in nonprofits or cultural institutions. * Understanding of Content Management System (CMS) platforms (Drupal, WordPress). * Familiarity with accessibility standards and responsive design. * Excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills. * Ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. * Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to work well with a wide range of staff and consultants. * Acute attention to detail and strong organizational skills. * Understanding of UX principles and experience with core web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) are a plus. In addition to offering competitive wages, the Museum’s benefits package includes: * Medical insurance – 2 plan options; Museum pays 75% of premium * Dental and vision insurance * Flexible spending account * 401(k) – Museum matches 50% of employee contribution up to 6%; employer contribution full vested after 3 years of employment * Life insurance and AD&D - $15,000 policy employer paid; additional life and AD&D available * Long term disability insurance * Paid vacation and sick leave, 10 paid holidays per year * Free parking * Tuition assistance and professional development * Employee assistance program The National WWII Museum is an Equal Opportunity Employer and seeks diversity in its workforce. We are dedicated to a policy of non-discrimination in employment on any basis including age, sex, race, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, or disability. Consistent with the Americans with Disabilities Act, applicants may request accommodations needed to participate in the application process.

jobs.title: Accessibility Tester
jobs.description: JPS Tech Solutions LLC **Accessibility Tester** **Location:** US-CO-Denver **Jobcode:**3627727 [Email Job](https://www.jobvertise.com/jobs/mailfriend?jobid=38255646&memberID=techfetch&title=Accessibility+Tester&tofriend=1) | [Report Job](https://www.jobvertise.com/job/38255646/accessibility_tester_jobs_in_denver_co.html#) Role: Tester Location: Denver, Colorado 80203- Remote Interview Type: Either Webcam or In Person Experience: 6+ Years Note: Candidates must be located Colorado Candidates willing to move to Colorado for this position will not be considered. Job Summary: The Accessibility Tester will support the Office of Information Technology (OIT) applications by ensuring they comply with accessibility standards and provide an inclusive experience for individuals with disabilities. Project Goals: * Identify accessibility issues in assigned OIT applications. * Ensure compliance with accessibility standards such as WCAG 2.1 AA and Section 508. * Provide actionable recommendations for remediation of identified accessibility barriers. Key Responsibilities: Perform comprehensive accessibility testing, including: * Manual testing using assistive technologies (screen readers, keyboard navigation, etc. * Automated accessibility testing using appropriate tools. * Reviewing design specifications and wireframes for accessibility. * Report accessibility defects with detailed reproduction steps and recommended solutions. * Collaborate with development, business, and QA teams to resolve accessibility issues. * Participate in accessibility review meetings and training sessions as needed. * Deliver detailed accessibility test plans and test reports. * Document accessibility best practices and guidelines. * Maintain up-to-date knowledge of accessibility standards. Deliverables: * Accessibility test plans. * Accessibility test reports (including severity and recommendations * Defect reports in OIT's defect tracking system. * Summaries of findings and progress updates. Reporting: Directly report to Christine Coakley, Manager Enterprise Applications. Provide regular status updates through SH weekly notes. Acceptance Criteria: Deliverables will be reviewed and approved by OIT's designated representative to ensure they meet requirements and quality standards. Qualifications: * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Solid understanding of OOPS concepts. * Hands-on experience with API and Webservice Testing. * Good understanding of traceability matrix, test strategy, and test planning. * Familiarity with Agile methodology and Jira process. Platforms: Java, Mobile-PWA, Salesforce. Duties & Responsibilities: * Perform hands-on testing. * Write detailed test scenarios and provide estimates for test planning. * Review technical and business documents to prepare test cases. * Follow accessibility checklist for the testing team. * Document all findings and communicate effectively with stakeholders. * Provide expert advice on accessibility best practices and remediation strategies. * Adhere to project timelines and OIT quality standards.

jobs.title: Accessibility Lead Tester
jobs.description: 🚀 We’re Hiring: Accessibility Lead Tester 🚀 Experience – 8 to 12 Yrs Location: Bangalore/Hyderabad | Band: U4 Shift: 1:30 PM - 10:30 PM (2nd Shift Support) Are you an expert in accessibility testing with a passion for ensuring digital inclusivity? Join us as an Accessibility Lead Tester in the retail domain and help us build accessible web and mobile experiences for all users, including those with disabilities. We need someone with deep expertise in accessibility standards, testing tools, and cross-functional collaboration! Key Responsibilities 🔹 Conduct comprehensive accessibility audits for retail web and mobile apps using both manual and automated tools 🔹 Test retail customer journeys with assistive technologies (screen readers like JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver, TalkBack, etc.) 🔹 Ensure compliance with WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA and guide the remediation of accessibility issues 🔹 Work closely with designers, developers, and PMs to build inclusive retail experiences 🔹 Provide mentorship and promote best accessibility practices within the team 🔹 Drive accessibility compliance for emerging retail technologies like ARIA, HTML5, CSS, JS, PDF, video/media content 🔹 Report accessibility findings and work with stakeholders to resolve issues Required Qualifications 🔹 8–12 years of experience in accessibility testing (3-5 years with web and mobile apps) 🔹 Strong expertise in accessibility tools: AXE, WAVE, MS Insights, JIRA 🔹 Hands-on experience with assistive technologies (screen readers, magnifiers, voice control systems) 🔹 In-depth knowledge of WCAG, ADA, Section 508, VPAT/ACR 🔹 Proven experience working with cross-functional teams in an agile environment 🔹 Relevant certifications (CPACC, WAS, Trusted Tester) are a plus Preferred Skills 🔹 Retail domain experience: E-commerce platforms, POS systems, loyalty apps 🔹 Understanding of international accessibility laws Why Join Us? We value diversity, equity, and inclusion. Help us make the digital world accessible to all, regardless of their abilities. Become a part of a team that is dedicated to providing accessible experiences across the retail sector. 📥 Apply now: [email protected]

jobs.title: Accessibility Engineer
jobs.description: Are you passionate about accessibility and building inclusive experiences? Excellent! We are looking for an experienced Accessibility Engineer contractor to join our in-house accessibility team. Solving for inclusion requires empathy with users, building influential relationships, proposing bold ideas, and introducing new processes that integrate accessibility thinking into our product development. Your work will have a high impact as you partner with other engineers, designers, and user researchers to build the tools, processes, and frameworks needed to ensure Spotify experiences are accessible and inclusive for all our millions of users. **What You’ll Do** - Work day-to-day with an agile team of engineers, designers, user researchers, and product managers to build and maintain our central approach to accessibility - Collaborate with other engineering teams on code reviews, evolving our shared processes and tooling to support many teams working on Spotify's exciting range of products - Propose and lead initiatives that help achieve our goal of scaling accessibility and ultimately making the Spotify experience more inclusive - Educate, inspire, and influence fellow Spotifiers by sharing your knowledge and experience of accessibility through Spotify-specific guidelines and accessibility training based on findings from user research - Drive the adoption of accessibility across the company by partnering and occasionally embedding with our product teams as they update existing and build exciting new features - Find innovative approaches, improve the developer experience, and remove any friction teams may have when building accessible Spotify experiences. **Who You Are** - A fullstack engineer with experience making web or mobile apps, tools or features accessible - You have good knowledge and understanding of WCAG criteria and accessibility best practices - You are aware of the needs and challenges people with disabilities face when using software - You understand and are able to articulate the difference between web accessibility and mobile accessibility - You are comfortable using assistive technologies such as screen readers and accessibility features across various platforms (incl. VoiceOver, Talkback, Browser extensions, JAWS, and NVDA) - You have experience auditing and testing digital products for accessibility - You enjoy mentoring and supporting peers and engineering teams, fostering the best engineering and accessibility practices - You are eager to learn new technologies (we will support you with training & mentoring) **Other Information :** **Start Date :** 2026-02-10 **End Date :** 2026-08-31 **Application Deadline** : 2026-02-08 **Work Model :** On-site

We are looking for an experienced Accessibility Engineer contractor to join our in-house accessibility team. Solving for inclusion requires empathy with users, building influential relationships, proposing bold ideas, and introducing new processes that integrate accessibility thinking into our product development. Your work will have a high impact as you partner with other engineers, designers, and user researchers to build the tools, processes, and frameworks needed to ensure Spotify experiences are accessible and inclusive for all our millions of users. **What You’ll Do** - Work day-to-day with an agile team of engineers, designers, user researchers, and product managers to build and maintain our central approach to accessibility - Collaborate with other engineering teams on code reviews, evolving our shared processes and tooling to support many teams working on Spotify's exciting range of products

Propose and lead initiatives that help achieve our goal of scaling accessibility and ultimately making the Spotify experience more inclusive Educate, inspire, and influence fellow Spotifiers by sharing your knowledge and experience of accessibility through Spotify-specific guidelines and accessibility training based on findings from user research Find innovative approaches, improve the developer experience, and remove any friction teams may have when building accessible Spotify experiences. A fullstack engineer with experience making web or mobile apps, tools or features accessible You have experience auditing and testing digital products for accessibility

jobs.title: Web Accessibility Specialist
jobs.description: Trigyn's direct government client has an immediate need for a Web Accessibility Specialist in Albany, NY. Description: The department requires an IT Specialist with Web Accessibility expertise and auditing to assist with System Transformation projects. • Will perform extensive accessibility analysis and create documentation for new or existing transactions that will involve: • Working independently or with accessibility testers to assess web pages and documents (i.e., PDF) for conformance to established web accessibility standards (in particular WCAG 2.0 AA). • Developing detailed reports explaining accessibility defects identified through an audit and suggest remediation methods/techniques. • Reviewing results of assessment with developers, project team members, and management. • Answering questions regarding testing methods and interpretation of the results. • Creating comprehensive accessibility documentation for use by developers. • Guidance and participation in developing an accessibility audit methodology. • Participate in accessibility feedback to transaction and form designs. Mandatory Qualification: • Works in the field of Information Technology, experienced in the usage and support of a collection of development platforms, technical architectures or business applications and products that run on those platforms - beyond that of a Programmer. • Candidate is able to work independently, without assistance and provides guidance to others. May have an advanced education. • 72 months experience performing Web Accessibility Audits using assistive or automated technologies, such as JAWS, WAVE from WebAIM, NVDA, ZoomText, Dragon Naturally Speaking, Adobe Acrobat, and Microsoft Accessibility Checker. • 60 months experience coding with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. • 60 months experience in UI Accessibility testing. • 72 months experience in developing technical documentation. • 60 months experience performing Accessibility Audits against Digital Documents (e.g., PDF, Word, Web Pages). • 72 months experience using and following Section 508 and Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI) guidelines and specifications–W3C Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.0 A and AA), Accessible Rich Internet Applications (ARIA), User Agent Accessibility Guidelines (UAAG), and Authoring Tool Accessibility Guidelines (ATAG). For Immediate Response call 732-640-2404, or send your resume to [email protected] TRIGYN TECHNOLOGIES, INC. is an EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER and has been in business for 35 years. TRIGYN is an ISO 27001:2022 and CMMI Level 5 certified company.

jobs.title: Student Accessibility Assistant
jobs.description: LSA Advancement Title II Accessibility Project Student Accessibility Assistant Job Posting ------------------------------------------- LSA Advancement is seeking detail-oriented, motivated students to join our Title II Document Accessibility Team. This initiative supports the university’s commitment to digital accessibility and an inclusive campus community. Key Responsibilities: * Complete the provided training on digital accessibility standards and tools. * Review and remediate (edit) Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides) and Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) documents to meet digital accessibility standards (including WCAG 2.1 AA). * Utilize accessibility checkers and U-M best practices for structure, color contrast, tagging, alternative text, and readable layout. * Collaborate with content owners and team members to resolve accessibility barriers. * Contribute to documentation and tutorials to promote accessibility awareness across our team. * Available for staff meetings. Qualifications: * Current University of Michigan student (undergraduate or graduate). * Attention to detail and strong organizational skills. * Strong interpersonal communication skills. * Familiarity with Google Workspace and Microsoft Office applications. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Students must have **work-study** to be eligible for this position. $15.00 per hour. jobs.title: Software Engineer III, Accessibility Infrastructure, People with disabilities (English) — Google Careers
jobs.specific_location: São Paulo, State Of São Paulo, Brazil,BRAZIL
jobs.description: Google São Paulo, State of São Paulo, Brazil Please submit your resume in English - we can only consider applications submitted in this language. Google welcomes people with disabilities. Only applications of candidates with disabilities will be evaluated for this role in compliance with the provisions of Article 93 Federal Labor Law 8.213/91. Please provide documentation that attest your disability. Please merge it in your resume PDF. O Google tem orgulho em ser um local de trabalho inclusivo. Somente as candidaturas de Pessoas com Deficiência (PcD) serão avaliadas para essa vaga, em conformidade com o disposto no artigo 93 da Lei Federal do Trabalho de número 8.213/91. Pedimos, por gentileza, que a pessoa candidata envie o currículo em inglês, e forneça documentação que comprove sua deficiência. A documentação pode ser enviada junto ao currículo, no mesmo PDF. ### Minimum qualifications: * Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. * Documentation or certification that attests to your disability. * 2 years of experience with software development in one or more programming languages (e.g., C++, Go or Java), or 1 year of experience with an advanced degree in an industry setting. * Ability to communicate in English fluently in order to support global teams. ### Preferred qualifications: * Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or a related technical field. * Experience developing accessible technologies. * Experience writing and maintaining software tests, designing systems, and working developer infrastructure. * Excellent collaboration and communication skills, particularly working in a team setting or cross-team/cross-functional role. ### About the job Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward. The mission of Products for All (P4A) is to ensure that Google’s products and services build toward a world. Our engineering efforts are currently focused on the needs of users with disabilities. Through solutions that support Web, Android, and iOS, we build the foundational standard, tools, services, and partnerships that simplify, measure, and ensure verifiable accessibility at every stage of the product development lifecycle across Google, making it easy for Google teams to meet and exceed accessibility requirements. The Core team builds the technical foundation behind Google’s flagship products. We are owners and advocates for the underlying design elements, developer platforms, product components, and infrastructure at Google. These are the essential building blocks for excellent, safe, and coherent experiences for our users and drive the pace of innovation for every developer. We look across Google’s products to build central solutions, break down technical barriers and strengthen existing systems. As the Core team, we have a mandate and a unique opportunity to impact important technical decisions across the company. ### Responsibilities * Review code developed by other developers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency). * Triage product or system issues and debug/track/resolve by analyzing the sources of issues and the impact on hardware, network, or service operations and quality. * Contribute to tools that help other Google engineers understand, diagnose, and fix accessibility issues in their codebase. * Write product or system development code and develop integrations between our core tooling and other company-wide systems. * Build and apply domain expertise, scaling accessibility by participation in P4A's accessibility consulting and educational programs. Information collected and processed as part of your Google Careers profile, and any job applications you choose to submit is subject to Google's [Applicant and Candidate Privacy Policy](https://www.google.com/about/careers/applications/privacy-policy). Google is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to building a workforce that is representative of the users we serve, creating a culture of belonging, and provid

jobs.title: Product Manager, Web Experience
jobs.description: The Jed Foundation (JED) is a nonprofit organization that protects emotional health and prevents suicide for our nation’s teens and young adults. JED seeks a highly motivated and experienced Product Manager to join our team. Reporting to the Vice President of Digital Products, the Product Manager will guide the strategy, execution, and performance of one of its most critical digital assets: JedFoundation.org. This position requires a highly skilled and experienced individual who is passionate about helping organizations grow and deliver impact through digital products and experiences. Initially, you will lead a team through the comprehensive redesign of JedFoundation.org to better serve each of its diverse audiences and organizational objectives. Following the launch, you will guide the product’s ongoing strategy and implementation to ensure it effectively supports JED's growth goals. You will be responsible for balancing three key priorities: delivering free, evidence-based resources through our Mental Health Resource Center; driving revenue through data-driven online fundraising; and growing the reach of JED's programs by generating qualified leads for our school, community, and policy/advocacy initiatives Key Responsibilities -------------------- **Website Redesign & Launch (Year 1)** * **Discovery & Strategy:** Guide the discovery, research, and strategic planning for the full-scale redesign of JedFoundation.org, aligning all stakeholders on project goals, KPI’s, scope, and audience needs. * **Project Leadership:** Act as the central owner for the redesign project, managing the timeline, budget, and relationships with internal teams (Marketing, Programs, Development) and external vendors (design and development agencies). * **Collaborate on Product Design and Prototyping:** Championing a user-centric and data-informed approach, you’ll partner with product designers in the co-creation, prototyping and testing of new site designs. You’ll support the design team in creating intuitive, engaging, and highly accessible (WCAG compliant) user journeys, along with a scalable UI design system for implementation. This includes working with youth and other key stakeholders to co-design solutions that deliver critical value for both JED and its users. * **Requirements & Execution:** Translate stakeholder needs and user research into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria for the engineering team. * **QA Testing and Launch Preparation**: Oversee the content migration, QA testing, and go-live procedure to mitigate risk of SEO loss and avoid introducing usability or performance issues. * **Launch and Change Management:** Work with internal staff and external partners to facilitate the deployment . Coordinate training and documentation support to ensure seamless change management. Establish baseline KPIs and measurement plans to evaluate the new site's success. **Ongoing Product Management & Strategy (Post-Launch)** * **Agile Leadership:** Guide a cross-department team through continuous optimization and scaling of this product by championing a data-informed, iterative process where user feedback and analytics drive a shared roadmap of feature enhancements, growth experiments, fixes and ongoing maintenance. Develop and maintain product documentation, including product manuals, release notes, user guides, and FAQs. * **Data-Driven Decisions:** Continuously analyze site performance using BI and data analytics tools to identify optimization opportunities and guide the ongoing implementation of data-informed product improvements. Collaborate with data and evaluation teams to ensure JED stakeholders and constituents have access to key data for reporting and growth. Maintain up-to-date knowledge of digital product trends and emerging technologies, and incorporate these into product management strategies. * **Vendor Managemen**t: Collaborate with external partners and ensure our digital products are developed and maintained according to best practices and industry standards. * **Product Roadmap:** Develop, own, and communicate the long-term product roadmap for JedFoundation.org, prioritizing features and enhancements that align with JED's strategic objectives. * **Oversee The Quality Assurance Lifecycle**: Refine and lead JED’s quality assurance testing protocols throughout the product lifecycle. Work with internal and external engineers, JED program staff, and external QA partners to ensure the site is performant and usable across audiences and devices. * **Stakeholder Alignment:** Act as the primary digital product expert, regularly reporting on performance, gathering feedback, and aligning cross-functional teams around a unified product vision. **Functional Optimization & Growth (Core Priorities)** * **Mental Health Resource Center:** Partner with JED's clinical and content teams to enhance the discoverability, accessibility, and impact of our free, evidence-based mental

Skills, Knowledge & Expertise ----------------------------- * **2-3 years of experience** in digital product management, with a proven track record of managing high-traffic, content-rich websites. * **Demonstrated experience leading a large-scale product redesign** from discovery through launch and iteration. * **Strong technical literacy,** including deep experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Contentful, Wordpress), BI/analytics and data infrastructure (eg: GA4, Looker, Snowflake), collaboration/design and testing tools (eg: Figjam, Maze) experimentation and user behavior tools (VWO, Hotjar, Qualtrics). * **Expertise in data-driven decision-making,** with a strong ability to conduct A/B tests, analyze user data, and translate insights into product requirements. * **Experience working in an agile/scrum** development environment, with a strong understanding of product owner responsibilities. * **Proven success in optimizing digital funnels** for specific conversion goals, such as online fundraising, e-commerce, or lead generation. * **Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills,** with the ability to unite diverse, non-technical teams around a common goal. * **Deep understanding of UX/UI principles** and a passionate commitment to web accessibility (WCAG) and creating inclusive digital experiences. * **Experience with SEO/SEM best practices** and marketing automation/CRM integrations (Salesforce

jobs.title: Senior UX Designer, Design System & Accessibility
jobs.description: SAS is a global leader in analytics software and services. We help organizations transform data into insights, driving business innovation and customer success. At SAS, we believe in the power of analytics to solve the world's most challenging problems. Our solutions empower businesses and governments to make better decisions, improve performance, and deliver exceptional value. Join our team and be part of a company that is shaping the future of analytics. About the role: SAS is seeking a talented and experienced Senior UX Designer to join our Design System and Accessibility team. In this role, you will play a crucial part in shaping the user experience of our products by contributing to our design system and advocating for accessibility best practices. You will collaborate with product teams, engineers, and other designers to ensure our products are intuitive, inclusive, and visually consistent. Responsibilities: Contribute to the development and maintenance of our design system, including UI components, patterns, and guidelines. Conduct user research, usability testing, and heuristic evaluations to identify user needs and pain points. Create wireframes, mockups, and prototypes to effectively communicate design concepts. Champion accessibility best practices, ensuring our products meet WCAG standards and are usable by everyone. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate the design system and accessibility principles into product development. Provide design guidance and support to product teams. Stay up-to-date with the latest UX trends, accessibility standards, and design system best practices. Qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Human-Computer Interaction, Interaction Design, Graphic Design, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. 5+ years of professional experience in UX design, with a focus on design systems and accessibility. Proficiency in design tools such as Figma, Sketch, or Adobe XD. Strong understanding of user-centered design principles, usability best practices, and accessibility standards (WCAG). Experience creating and maintaining design system components and documentation. Excellent communication, collaboration, and presentation skills. Portfolio showcasing relevant UX design work, including examples of design system contributions and accessibility considerations. Preferred Qualifications: Experience with front-end development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript). Familiarity with agile development methodologies. Experience working in a large enterprise software environment. SAS is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law. SAS provides a barrier-free workplace and embraces all qualified applicants with varied perspectives and backgrounds. If you’re a US applicant with a qualified disability or a disabled veteran, you can request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use www.sas.com/careers. For assistance, please call +1-919-531-7808 or email [email protected] . If you are a candidate from CA, CO, IL, MD, MA, MN, NY, NJ, VT, HI, or WA who has applied to an open position, and you have questions on Pay Transparency, please email [email protected] indicating the requisition to which you've applied. SAS is committed to protecting its candidates' privacy throughout the recruiting process and uses the iCIMS Platform to process recruiting related information. You will be presented with the SAS Global Privacy Statement for Job Applicants when applying and must review and acknowledge the terms to proceed. To request deletion of your Candidate Profile for previously submitted applications, please e-mail [email protected] from the same e-mail account you used to create your Candidate Profile.

jobs.title: Senior UI Designer/Web Developer
jobs.description: **Job Posting Title:** Senior UI Designer/Web Developer **----** **Hiring Department:** Applied Research Laboratories **----** **Position Open To:** All Applicants **----** **Weekly Scheduled Hours:** 40 **----** **FLSA Status:** Exempt **----** **Earliest Start Date:** Immediately **----** **Position Duration:** Expected to Continue **----** **Location:** PICKLE RESEARCH CAMPUS **----** **Job Details:** Purpose ------- Applied Research Laboratories’ (ARL:UT) Strategic Communications Office (SCO) is seeking a Senior UI Designer/Web Developer to shape and elevate the look, feel, and usability of ARL’s web presence. This senior-level role is ideal for an experienced designer/developer with an eye for visual design, front-end expertise and the ability to lead and mentor others. Responsibilities ---------------- Design Leadership & User Experience * Lead the visual design, user experience, and front-end standards for ARL’s web properties. * Translate feedback, user needs, and organizational goals into thoughtful design solutions. * Establish and maintain consistent branding, layout, and interaction patterns across platforms. * Keep ARL’s sites visually modern, intuitive, and engaging. * Guide and review design work produced by web staff to ensure quality and consistency. Web Development * Maintain ARL’s portfolio of internal and public-facing websites. * Implement and oversee front-end development using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. * Review code, provide technical guidance, and ensure best practices across the team. * Oversee and prioritize web requests through ServiceNow, ensuring timely, high-quality delivery. * Stay current with emerging web technologies and practices while adhering to ARL’s security and information assurance standards. Accessibility & Standards * Lead compliance efforts for accessibility standards (WCAG, Section 508). * Establish accessibility best practices and documentation for the web team. * Recommend training opportunities and provide guidance to staff on accessibility and inclusive design. Leadership & Collaboration * Supervise two web developers (one full-time, one part-time), providing technical direction, feedback, and professional development support. * Communicate and collaborate effectively with colleagues across departments to deliver web solutions that meet organizational needs. * Contribute to and support SCO department-wide initiatives alongside designers, communications specialists, and archivists. Required Qualifications ----------------------- * Bachelor’s degree. * Three or more years of experience across a combination of the following: web development, UI design, and UX design. * Proven experience leading web design or development projects from concept through implementation, with a portfolio demonstrating UI/UX design and front-end implementation. * Experience providing technical guidance, mentorship, or informal supervision. * Demonstrated proficiency in front-end coding (HTML, CSS, JavaScript). * Experience using GitLab (or other version control systems). * Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG, Section 508). * Ability to independently lead complex work while communicating effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders. US Citizen is required. Applicant selected will be subject to a government security investigation and must meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information at the level appropriate to the project requirements of the position. Preferred Qualifications ------------------------ * Experience supervising or formally managing web developers or designers. * Proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite, including Adobe XD. * Experience using ticketing/support platforms (ServiceNow or equivalent). General Notes ------------- An agency designated by the federal government handles the investigation as to the requirement for eligibility for access to classified information. Factors considered during this investigation include but are not limited to allegiance to the United States, foreign influence, foreign preference, criminal conduct, security violations, drug involvement, the likelihood of continuation of such conduct, etc. Please mark "yes" on the application question that asks if additional materials are required. Failure to attach all additional materials listed below may result in a delay in application processing. Visit our website ([www.arlut.utexas.edu](http://www.arlut.utexas.edu/)) for additional information about Applied Research Laboratories. UT Austin offers a competitive benefits package that includes: ·100% employer-paid basic medical coverage ·Retirement contributions ·Paid vacation and sick time ·Paid holidays Please visit our [Human Resources (HR) website](https://hr.utexas.edu/)to learn more about the total benefits offered. Salary Range ------------ $75,000-$85,000+/negotiable depending on qualifications. Working Conditions ------------------ * Standard office c

jobs.title: Senior Accessibility & QA Automation Test Engineer - Aretum
jobs.description: **About Aretum** Aretum is a mission-driven organization committed to delivering innovative, technology-enabled solutions to our customers across defense, civilian, and homeland security sectors. Our teams work at the intersection of strategy, technology, and transformation, helping agencies solve their most critical challenges. We believe in investing in our people and creating a culture where collaboration, inclusion, and professional growth are at the forefront. **Job Summary** The Senior Accessibility & QA Automation Test Engineer, is a hands-on test engineer responsible for ensuring enterprise-scale digital systems are functionally correct, usable, secure, performant, and fully accessible to all users, including individuals with disabilities.The Senior Accessibility & QA Automation Test Engineer operates independently, influences technical decisions, and raises the overall accessibility and quality maturity of Agile teams. Due to the nature of our work as a federal consulting organization, employees may be expected to handle Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) and must adhere to applicable safeguarding and compliance requirements. **Responsibilities** * Perform manual and automated functional testing of application code, APIs, and integrated services * Execute non-functional testing, including performance and load testing, to validate system stability and capacity * Conduct manual and automated accessibility testing throughout the software development lifecycle * Validate conformance with WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA requirements and applicable federal accessibility standards * Perform screen reader and keyboard-only testing using JAWS and NVDA * Execute automated accessibility scans using tools such as axe, pa11y, and ANDI, and validate results through manual testing * Develop, execute, and maintain test plans, test cases, and test scripts aligned with user stories and acceptance criteria * Integrate functional and accessibility tests into established CI/CD pipelines * Ensure test results are documented, version-controlled, and reported through approved tools * Clearly document defects, risks, and remediation guidance in issue-tracking systems * Collaborate closely with developers, UX designers, product owners, and accessibility leads to resolve issues efficiently * Participate fully in Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives * Proactively identify quality and accessibility risks and raise them early to support timely remediation Requirements ------------ * Bachelor’s degree (BA or BS) or equivalent combination of education and experience (an additional 4 years of experience may substitute) * 5+ years of experience in software testing, quality engineering, or SDET roles * Active DHS Trusted Tester Certification * Demonstrated experience performing manual and automated testing of web-based applications * Experience with test automation tools such as Selenium, Cypress, or Playwright * Experience with API testing tools such as Postman * Experience with performance and load testing tools such as NeoLoad or JMeter * Strong understanding of accessibility standards, including WCAG 2.1 and Section 508 concepts * Hands-on experience using accessibility testing tools, including axe, pa11y, ANDI, Color Contrast Analyzer, JAWS, and NVDA * Demonstrated experience performing manual accessibility testing using assistive technologies and screen readers * Experience working within CI/CD pipelines and automated testing environments * Experience working in Agile / Scrum or SAFe-based delivery environments * Strong verbal and written communication skills * Proven ability to collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience supporting large-scale, cloud-based, microservices-oriented systems * Experience producing accessibility testing documentation, summaries, or conformance evidence * Experience collaborating closely with UX and design system teams to validate accessible components and patterns **Travel Requirements** This is a remote position; however, occasional travel may be required based on project needs, client meetings, team collaboration events, or training sessions.Travel is expected to be less than 10% and will be communicated in advance whenever possible. **EEO Statement** _Aretum is committed to fostering a workplace rooted in excellence, integrity, and equal opportunity for all. We adhere to merit-based hiring practices, ensuring that all employment decisions are made based on qualifications, skills, and ability to perform the job, without preference or consideration of factors unrelated to job performance._ _As an Equal Opportunity Employer, Aretum complies with all applicable federal, state, and local employment laws._ _We are proud to support our nation’s veterans and military families,providing career opportunities that honor their service and

jobs.title: Program Manager, Accelerator
jobs.description: Access to Success Organization (ATS) is a Toronto-based not-for-profit on a mission to Enable Everyone. As a leading global innovation hub for accessibility startups, we help scale life-changing innovations to reach people with disabilities worldwide. We are hiring the next member of our leadership team, the Program Manager, to manage and grow our accelerator and partner programming. This is a highly self-driven leadership role where your work will directly impact the growth of the organization. You will lead program design, speaker recruitment and community management, and manage the day-to-day operations for one of the most unique accelerators in the country. Working closely with the Executive Director, you will have direct access to our global network of speakers, mentors, investors, and ecosystem leaders. You will be the face of the accelerator for our global community of accessibility entrepreneurs, and play a key role in their growth. **Format and timing:** Full-time (up to 40 hours/week) **Location:** Hybrid, up to 4 days/week at our downtown Toronto office **Compensation:** $80,000 - $90,000 per year based on qualifications **Start Date:**March 2, 2026 **Responsibilities:** * Design and deliver world-class programming for our accelerator and partner-backed innovation streams, including program design, speaker recruitment and milestone tracking * Keep track of the latest cutting-edge innovations in accessibility in Canada and around the world, including frequent conversations with entrepreneurs and traveling to relevant domestic and international conferences * Support entrepreneurs participating in the program with their acceleration needs * Recruit and manage speakers, mentors, and experts from across the global accessibility and innovation sectors by leveraging our network * Lead community management, including managing the online program hub, onboarding the new cohort, organizing group check-ins, managing participant and speaker communications, and ensure each startup gets what they need to thrive * Working closely with our Marketing Lead, support experience design, logistics and event hosting for major events like the Demo Day * Collaborate with the Executive Director on partnership activities, including planning and execution of activations with corporate and ecosystem partners **What we are looking for:** * 3+ years experience in accelerators, venture capital, entrepreneurship or innovation ecosystem development * You must have supported entrepreneurs and innovators, ideally through a structured program * Exceptionally strong community development and management skills * Experience with no-code tools like Airtable, Notion, Monday.com, etc. * A track record of managing external-facing programs and creating memorable experiences * Familiarity with accessibility and disability inclusion would be a strong asset **Important notes:** * People with disabilities are highly encouraged to apply! As a not-for-profit working for disability inclusion, lived experience with disabilities is a tangible skillset that will add to our mission * In the spirit of inclusivity, we will share most of the interview questions with the shortlisted candidates in advance - we want you to put your best foot forward! * We are a team of builders – if you have a knack for dreaming up and implementing off-the-beaten-path ideas, you will fit in very well. * You must be able to work in-person from our office in Downtown Toronto up to 4 days per week and attend community events throughout the year. Some events may take place after business hours. If interviewees can be asked to provide references, employers should too. Here are ours: * Our Fall 2021 Intern: Lessons from my dream job (https://medium.com/inclusive-by-design/lessons-from-my-dream-job-a81d6851eaf4) * Our 2025 Marketing Lead: “Being part of the Access to Success team was a highly rewarding experience that provided a unique opportunity to work hands-on in the accessibility innovation ecosystem. Working alongside a passionate and purpose-driven team made the experience even more meaningful and impactful.” **Applications will be considered on a rolling basis. Please apply here:**https://airtable.com/appL3m8W7amcBuKl1/pagCleY2okWLQrpve/form

jobs.title: Part Time Educator (Accessibility & Inclusion focus)
jobs.description: Lincoln Park Zoo inspires communities to create environments where wildlife will thrive in our urbanizing world. The zoo is a leader in local and global conservation, animal care and welfare, learning, and science. A historic Chicago landmark founded in 1868, the not-for-profit Lincoln Park Zoo, is a privately-managed, member-supported organization and is free and open 365 days a year. Visit us at lpzoo.org. ### GENERAL SUMMARY The Part Time Educator (Accessibility & Inclusion focus) is responsible for guiding learner-centered experiences both on zoo grounds and in virtual programs for guests with disabilities. Under the direction of the Hart Prins Fund Accessibility & Inclusion Manager, this individual will provide meaningful experiences that connect guests to wildlife, utilizing best practices in both informal learning and accessibility and inclusion. This position is responsible for conducting the majority of Lincoln Park Zoo’s A&I programming. The ideal candidate is able to work with a variety of people in a collaborative fashion with an eye towards quality programming and excellent guest experience **This is a part-time position at 24 hours per week. The position runs from April 1 – Nov. 25, 2026. Candidates must be available on weekends and evenings.** ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS ----------------------- * Design, prepare and deliver programs that align to the zoo’s learning framework and support learning variation, including: * Preparing a written program plan in advance * Independently or collaboratively facilitating programs, such as Sensory-Friendly Mornings, customized group visits, and more. * Setting up and breaking down all materials for programs which they facilitate. * Provide flexible accessibility for a wide range of disabilities and support guests with appropriate accommodations during programs. * Work with guests of all age levels and abilities. * Manage groups on zoo grounds during busy visitation periods. * Support the Hart Prins Fund Accessibility & Inclusion Manager during select programs he leads. * Foster a welcoming and inclusion space for learning. * Support other Learning programs as needed. * Other duties as assigned. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES ----------------------------- * Ability to work with groups on zoo grounds and in a virtual environment. * Ability to adapt to a rapidly changing environment and guest needs. * Ability to expect variation in learning, communication, and interactions with guests and pivot to support them in the moment. * Ability to interact comfortably with culturally and linguistically diverse guests and guests with disabilities. * Ability to work in a team and problem solve to ensure timely completion of all projects and programs. * Ability to interact courteously and respectfully with supervisors, fellow employees, volunteers, zoo visitors and general public. * Have reliable transportation. * Dependable and punctual. * American Sign Language fluency is a plus. EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS ---------------------- * Preferred candidates will have high school degree or GED. EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS ----------------------- * 1-3 years’ experience working with people who have disabilities. * 1-3 years’ experience with informal and/or formal education, early childhood, performance, cultural interpretation, museum studies, biology, conversation, or related fields. PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS --------------------- * Capable of independently moving across zoo grounds, remaining stationary for long periods of time, and working in a variety of weather conditions. * Must be able to move equipment on a rolling cart or in wagon. ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS ----------------------- * Available to work weekends, evenings, and special events which may occur outside of regular work schedule. * Ability to pass a background check. OUR PROMISE Lincoln Park Zoo is committed to an environment that is inclusive and welcoming. We celebrate the diverse qualities, perspectives, values, and experiences of all people. For Wildlife. For All. LINCOLN PARK ZOO BENEFITS In addition to such standard benefits ( Blue Cross Blue Shield) as medical, dental, vision, life, and disability coverages, and a 403(b) savings plan with an institutional match, benefits of working at the zoo include (benefits are for regular full time employees): * Free admission to many other Chicago cultural institutions * Free on-site parking anytime with access to Lincoln Park, Lakeshore Trail, and North Avenue Beach * Discounts on event tickets (free admission to select events), attractions, dining, and retail at the zoo * Quarterly staff mingle events and “LPZooU” training and educational sessions about the zoo, wildlife, ecology, and related subjects * Company contribution to Health Savings Account (Medical PPO only) * A working environment with green spaces and regularly available visits with wildlife! **HOURLY WAGE: $23.70/HOUR** Apply for Part Time Educator (acce

jobs.title: Digital Technology Accessibility Domain Leader
jobs.description: At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. We do this by driving Responsible Growth and delivering for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day. Being a Great Place to Work is core to how we drive Responsible Growth. This includes our commitment to being an inclusive workplace, attracting and developing exceptional talent, supporting our teammates’ physical, emotional, and financial wellness, recognizing and rewarding performance, and how we make an impact in the communities we serve. Bank of America is committed to an in-office culture with specific requirements for office-based attendance and which allows for an appropriate level of flexibility for our teammates and businesses based on role-specific considerations. At Bank of America, you can build a successful career with opportunities to learn, grow, and make an impact. Join us! **Job Description:** This job is responsible for maximizing the value for one or more products. Key responsibilities include working with stakeholders to understand their needs, and with product managers or specialists to ensure they are aligning on priorities, communicating the product vision and roadmap to stakeholders and the team, and driving user adoption. Job expectations include ensuring delivery of products that meet client expectations, including features, security, availability, resiliency, timelines and costs. **Responsibilities:** * Generates the vision and roadmap for the product to align with strategic direction for the business or technology domain * Communicates the product vision and roadmap to stakeholders and the team, and drives user adoption * Collaborates with stakeholders to understand their needs, problems, and aligns them on priorities * Prioritizes and creates work for multiple teams and works with cross-functional teams * Creates, prioritizes and refines stories in the product backlog * Reviews and accepts stories and makes decisions regarding scope and requirements * Partners with the team to ensure that optimum value is obtained through technology and through subject-matter expertise of the business **Managerial Responsibilities:** This position may also have responsibilities for managing associates. At Bank of America, all managers at this level demonstrate the following responsibilities, in addition to those specific to the role, listed above. * Opportunity & Inclusion Champion: Models an inclusive environment for employees and clients, aligned to company Great Place to Work goals. * Manager of Process & Data: Demonstrates deep process knowledge, operational excellence and innovation through a focus on simplicity, data based decision making and continuous improvement. * Enterprise Advocate & Communicator: Communicates enterprise decisions, purpose, and results, and connects to team strategy, priorities and contributions. * Risk Manager: Ensures proper risk discipline, controls and culture are in place to identify, escalate and debate issues. * People Manager & Coach: Provides inspection, coaching and feedback to motivate, differentiate and improve performance. * Financial Steward: Actively manages expenses and budgets in alignment with objectives, making sound financial decisions. * Enterprise Talent Leader: Assesses talent and builds bench strength for roles across the organization. * Driver of Business Outcomes: Delivers results by effectively prioritizing, inspecting and appropriately delegating team work. **Skills:** * Agile Practices * Influence * Result Orientation * Solution Delivery Process * Technical Strategy Development * Analytical Thinking * Collaboration * DevOps Practices * Risk Management * Stakeholder Management * Application Development * Business Acumen * Data Management * Financial Management * Solution Design jobs.title: Digital Accessibility Expert (Remote)
jobs.description: We are seeking an experienced, pragmatic Digital Accessibility Expert to provide hands-on support across the university while the certified specialist is on leave and key team members are retiring. This role is not about building new software and more about enabling people and processes: helping staff and faculty understand accessibility expectations, interpret assessments (VPATs), and drive practical improvement at scale. * Please Note: * This is a 6-month engagement, with strong possibility of extension. * Must support at least 4-hour overlap with US-based team in US-Central Time (CT) ****Key Responsibilities:****_****Accessibility Governance & VPAT Review:****_ * Review VPATs and other accessibility documentation for ICT/enterprise systems e.g. Oracle, learning management systems, and documents. * Interpret automated/AI-based assessments and validate them with expert judgment. * Develop and document alternative access plans for non-compliant systems (e.g., interim accommodations, remediation roadmaps). * Collaborate with security and procurement teams to “piggyback” on the annual security re‑attestation process and integrate accessibility checks. _****Barrier & Issue Management:****_ * Triage and respond to user-submitted accessibility barrier reports (users unable to access content/systems). * Diagnose issues, prioritize them, and coordinate with system/content owners on remediation plans.. * Track decisions, action plans, and progress in a structured, auditable way. _****Training, Communication & Evangelism:****_ * Design and deliver accessible, engaging training (live and recorded) for faculty and staff on: * Core accessibility principles (WCAG, inclusive design). * Creating accessible documents, presentations, and digital content (e.g., Word, PowerPoint, PDFs). * Using templates and tools correctly so they remain accessible. * Create and maintain clear, reusable materials (guides, checklists, FAQs, slide decks, templates). * Act as a visible, approachable champion for accessibility—building relationships and trust rather than “policing” compliance. _****Process & Tooling Support:****_ * Contribute to the design and refinement of university-wide accessibility processes (e.g., content remediation workflows, AI-based assessment pipelines). * Work with existing teams (IT, Public Affairs, Teaching & Learning) on: * Website accessibility and legacy content remediation. * LMS (Canvas) accessibility practices (in collaboration with the dedicated LMS team). * Digital signage and social media accessibility (with Public Affairs). * Provide informed feedback on selection and configuration of AI and other tools used for large-scale remediation (e.g., 70,000+ PDFs). _****Change Management & Culture Building:****_ * Support the change management team in shifting habits and mindsets around accessibility. * Tailor messaging and approaches to different stakeholder groups in a highly autonomous, faculty‑driven environment. * Promote accessibility as part of the client’s values and mission, not only as a compliance obligation. ****Required Experience & Skills:****_****Accessibility Expertise:****_ * Strong working knowledge of WCAG and related digital accessibility standards. * Hands-on experience assessing accessibility of: * Enterprise and web-based systems. * Digital documents (Word, PowerPoint, PDFs) and web content. * Experience interpreting VPATs (or equivalent) and translating them into clear risk and remediation guidance. _****Pragmatic Problem-Solving:****_ * Ability to balance strict standards with practical, context-aware solutions. * Comfortable providing recommendations that are both compliant and realistic given constraints (time, tools, staffing). * Experience designing or advising on alternative access plans where full compliance is not immediately feasible. _****Training & Communication:****_ * Proven experience delivering training and presentations to non-technical audiences. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English. * Ability to explain complex accessibility concepts in simple, actionable terms. * Experience creating accessible templates and guidance, and teaching others how to use them correctly. _****Stakeholder Management:****_ * Experience working in environments with high autonomy and “rogue actors” (e.g., universities, research, government). * Strong relationship-building skills; able to influence without authority. * Comfortable working across multiple departments with varied goals and constraints. _****General:****_ * Experience with AI- or tool-assisted accessibility assessments is a plus. * Familiarity with higher education environments is highly desirable. * Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and operate under time pressure driven by external mandates. ****Reporting & Collaboration:**** * Work closely with the Change Management & Accessibility lead, IT leadership (inc
